programme.desc: Crime drama spin-off, starring Patricia Arquette as Special Agent Avery Ryan, head of the FBI's cyber crime division. Crime drama, starring David Caruso and Emily Procter.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Teenager Lydia Wade manages to escape a gang of masked men trying to abduct her and her friend Rosie, the daughter of a US Navy commander. Warren Clarke and Colin Buchanan star, with Keith Barron.(n)

programme.title: Dalziel & Pascoe
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. A car crash leaves Dalziel suspended from duty pending an investigation into drink-driving allegations. Pascoe probes the source of the claims against his boss, and exposes a web of conspiracy surrounding the violent death of an elderly Army veteran - all the while coping with being a dad of the Nineties and dealing with his senile father-in-law. Warren Clarke and Colin Buchanan star.(n)

programme.title: Football, Prince William and Our Mental Health
programme.desc: Documentary following the Duke of Cambridge's work to break the taboo surrounding mental health, using football as a way to encourage men to open up and talk about their emotions. The documentary follows the Duke as he meets players and fans from grassroots to the elite of the game, and openly discusses their mental health challenges. Former England goalkeeper Joe Hart talks about how he has learnt to cope with difficulties at the very top of the game, and William hears from a group of bereaved fathers who use their local football team as a support network and safe space to talk. Former Premier League footballer Marvin Sordell opens up about his struggles with depression, while Chelsea manager Frank Lampard compares life now with his early experiences of professional football.(n)

However, they face another tough task against the Red Devils, who are yet to concede a goal in the competition this term. With analysis from Alan Shearer and Ian Wright.(n)

programme.title: Thierry Henry: My France, My Euros
programme.desc: The former France striker and Arsenal's all-time leading scorer looks at European Championship stories, assessing the social and cultural impact football has across the continent. As Henry's home country prepares to host Euro 2016 following last November's terror attacks in Paris, he explores how the beautiful game can unify a nation, from the success of the Netherlands in 1988 to Euro 96 in England, when 'football came home'. Contributors include Cesc Fabregas, Peter Schmeichel, Ruud Gullit, Jurgen Klinsmann, Alan Shearer, David Baddiel and Ian Broudie.(n)

programme.title: McFarland, USA (2015)
programme.desc: A former American football coach takes a job at an underprivileged school with a mostly Hispanic student body, and finds he has a lot to learn about their lives. When he discovers many of his pupils have a talent for running, he forms an athletics team and leads them to glory. Fact-based drama, starring Kevin Costner and Ramiro Rodriguez.(n)

programme.title: Your Home Made Perfect
programme.desc: Laura Jane Clark and Robert Jamison create virtual reality renovations for a three-bed home in Surrey, which has a poorly positioned front door and a cluttered hallway. The owners are divided over whether an extension would solve their problems, and the architects present them with two very different visions for what their home could be. Laura takes a characteristically pragmatic approach with a simple but striking contemporary design. But experimental architect Robert decides a far more radical approach is the only answer.(n)

programme.title: The Victorian Slum
programme.desc: The volunteers experience the living and working conditions of the 1890s, with cheap foodstuffs and mass-manufactured goods having found their way into the slum's shop - but only some of the residents can afford them. The Howarths' relative prosperity means Mandy can turn her attention to being a respectable Victorian house wife, while the Potters are offered a lifeline by their neighbour Maria, who needs help with her laundry business. Michael Mosley presents.(n)

programme.title: Thatcher: A Very British Revolution
programme.desc: The concluding part of the documentary looks at how Margaret Thatcher's leadership style and the issues of Europe and the poll tax split her cabinet and created public anger, leading to her downfall. Protests against the poll tax resulted in violence in central London, but more damagingly there was widespread disenchantment in Tory strongholds across the country. Fractures emerge with senior cabinet colleagues, worsened by her European policy and Geoffrey Howe's resignation leads to a leadership challenge.(n)

programme.title: Remarkable Places to Eat
programme.desc: Fred Sirieix travels to Austria's capital Vienna in the company of food writer Rachel Khoo. The duo begin in the city's first district, the epicentre of coffee house culture, a crucial part of the Viennese social scene. Here, Rachel takes Fred to the iconic Café Demel, known for its exquisite cakes. Moving on, they learn how to make a spinach, cream and bread dumpling, before meeting a trailblazing chef taking the city by storm. Fred in particular is wowed by his schnitzel, reworked into a haute cuisine masterpiece. Finally the duo end their evening with a visit to one of the city's many sausage stands.(n)

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig talks garbage as she asks Alan Davies and this week's guest panellists, Bridget Christie, Johnny Vegas and Holly Walsh, to reveal all they know about rubbish.(n)

programme.title: The First Team
programme.desc: As Cesare bids the club a fond farewell, Mark surprises the squad by announcing coach Chris Booth as caretaker manager - infuriating Petey, who cannot believe he has been passed over for the job. Mattie is finally selected to start a match, Benji employs a social media manager to increase his online followers and Chris Booth takes on a sports psychologist in an attempt to boost team performance.(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Russell Kane comperes an evening of stand-up at London's Hammersmith Apollo, treating the audience to his own brand of humour and introducing routines by comedy circuit stars Roisin Conaty and Nick Helm.(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Highlights of the latest Premier League fixtures - Chelsea v Manchester City at Stamford Bridge, Southampton v Arsenal at St Mary's Stadium and Burnley v Watford at Turf Moor. programme.title: Racism in the Ranks
programme.desc: Reporter Callum Tulley investigates allegations of bullying, harassment and discrimination in the British Army that come disproportionately from ethnic minority soldiers. He hears stories from ex-soldiers, some of whom were born in Commonwealth countries, and meets a soldier thrown out of the Army having shared racist material on an online group chat.(n)

programme.title: Dragons' Den: Best Ever Pitches
programme.desc: Joe Lycett narrates a collection of memorable moments in which contestants made unusual efforts to win over the panel, from serenading them to staging a zombie attack. The brains behind a musical gift service favours red roses and flattery, while the inventor of a voice coaching massage chair hopes his creation will relax them into submission. However, the show's most successful entrant, Levi Roots, knows the best way to the Dragons' wallets is through their stomachs.(n)

programme.title: Dragons' Den: Best Ever Pitches
programme.desc: Joe Lycett narrates a selection of memorable moments where members of the panel competed with each other to secure investment in a promising entrepreneur's product. Pitches featured include an umbrella dispensing machine which won the show's first ever investment, a travel toy company that all five panellists wanted a share of, and a commercial submarine business.(n)

programme.title: A House Through Time
programme.desc: David Olusoga explores the house's history from the 1880s to the First World War, a period that saw the property sliding down the social scale and turning from a family home to a multiple occupancy. He discovers how a resident responded to the death of his baby daughter by opening a milk bar for the temperance movement, only to get into legal trouble for watering down his product. David also discovers the story behind a mysterious occupant who lived in the house under a pseudonym and learns how the battle of Passchendaele changed the residents' lives forever.(n)

programme.title: Jay Blades' Home Fix
programme.desc: Aided by a rich catalogue of BBC home and garden archive, Jay offers tips on ways to tackle outstanding tasks and encourages viewers to become self-sufficient in their own homes.(n)

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: The dealers compete to buy an ornate clock by the distinguished British horologist Charles Frodsham, an antique tombola machine, a 19th-century armchair and a vintage coffee set.(n)

programme.title: Great American Railroad Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo begins a new journey on the tracks of the Ocean line to explore Canada's maritime provinces, en route to Quebec City. Along the way he dons a kilt and sporran to join in the recreation of an 18th-century Scottish Highland regiment and learns of a catastrophic explosion in Halifax harbour in 1917, which killed 2,000 people and left 25,000 homeless.(n)

programme.title: The Victorian Slum
programme.desc: In the concluding edition the slum dwellers move into the 20th century and find social change is in the air. Community spirit is embodied by the arrival of the co-operative movement and shopkeepers the Birds have members to look after rather than customers to profit from. The wider world continues to impact on life in the slum and some enjoy a day trip out to the countryside, while the men exercise their right to vote and the women learn what their East End counterparts did in the quest for female suffrage. Michael Mosley presents.(n)

In a fast-paced endgame, they attempt to steal one another's cases via tense head-to-head challenges - but only the player who gets over the finish line first will win what's inside their case. Game show, hosted by Dan Walker.(n)

programme.title: World Beaters

programme.title: Legends

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At Muncaster Castle in Cumbria, experts James Lewis and Adam Partridge set out to find the best antiques and collectibles. Presenter Paul Martin finds out about the legendary Tom Fool, the court jester who lived at the castle and caused mayhem during some of its most turbulent times.(n)

programme.title: Greatest Escapes to the Country

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's national and international events, with Kirsty Wark.(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: At Longmeadow, Monty Don shows how to prune spring-flowering roses and makes successional sowings of vegetables to ensure continuous crops. Adam Frost gives tips helping plants cope with drought conditions and shows how to save precious water. The programme meets two sisters from Dorset who have discovered a new love of gardening, supported in their efforts by many followers on social media, and in Bognor Regis visits a gardener who has used unusual materials and bargain plants to create her own dream garden. Plus, Frances Tophill heads to Hertfordshire to meet a couple of brothers whose gardens are designed with wildlife in mind and in.(n)

programme.title: The Glastonbury Experience Live
programme.desc: Jo Whiley and Mark Radcliffe celebrate the weekend that would have marked 50 years of the Glastonbury Festival with a blend of performances from the archives and live acoustic sets from contemporary artists. Included in the mix are previously unseen or rarely shown moments, including highlights from the Rolling Stones' headline set in 2013, PJ Harvey's rocking performance in 2004, the irrepressible Lizzo in an early acoustic performance in the BBC garden area, Florence + the Machine's debut on one of the main stages in 2010, Oasis on the NME Stage in 1994 plus Stormzy's headline set from last year.(n)

programme.title: Beyonce at Glastonbury 2011
programme.desc: Highlights of the Texas-born singer, record producer and actress's headline set on the Pyramid Stage, which brought the year's festival to a close. Her set spanned both her solo career and her time in Destiny's Child, incorporating snippets of covers from Eurythmics, Kings of Leon and Prince, with hits including Crazy in Love, Single Ladies (Put a Ring on It), If I Were a Boy and Best Thing I Never Had.(n)

programme.title: Glastonbury 2019
programme.desc: Memorable performances from Worthy Farm in Somerset, where artists including Stormzy, George Ezra, Bastille, Sheryl Crow, the Killers, Liam Gallagher, Janet Jackson, the Proclaimers, Kylie Minogue and Miley Cyrus entertained the enthusiastic crowds across the three days of the festival.(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Daniel De Simone investigates a global network of neo-Nazis that aims to destroy society, discovering that it is recruiting in the UK. Police say right-wing extremism is the fastest growing terrorist threat in the UK and that the coronavirus pandemic may be leaving young people vulnerable to radicalisation. As Daniel investigates he notices certain names cropping up again and again and tracks down some of the movement's most influential figures and reveals how the network operates across the globe.(n)

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham, Iolo Williams and Gillian Burke are live from their home patches, joined by presenter and natural history cameraman Gordon Buchanan who looks back on his epic wildlife week in Scotland. The team reveals a unique story of habitat rewilding taking place on the Knepp Estate in West Sussex where white storks are breeding in the wild, while Chris is on a mission to explain why people should all love wasps.(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.(n)

programme.title: Marrying Mum and Dad
programme.desc: Melvin Odoom and Naomi Wilkinson help a family of seven youngster plan their very own music festival wedding. Mum and dad think they ve got off easy until the paint begins to fly and they see their campervan assault course. However, with a surprise headline performance from Union J, it s looks this wedding could be a real smash hit.(n)

programme.title: My First
programme.desc: This episode follows Rory at his first-ever music festival.(n)

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Naomi Wilkinson and Tim Warwood check out a super-powered squirrel that can withstand the freezing temperatures of the Arctic winter.(n)

programme.title: All Over the Place
programme.desc: The very best of the Main Events, including Kent's World Custard Pie Championships.(n)

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: The team presents a music special, talking about the songs they love, and Mwaksy Mudenda attempts a sing and sign-language challenge. Coldplay's Chris Martin, saxophonist YolanDa and chart star Anne Marie reveal how viewers can get involved and Ella Henderson performs her new single.(n)

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve sets sail to Bali for a diving adventure to find a giant ocean sunfish.(n)

programme.title: Forces of Nature with Brian Cox
programme.desc: The physicist concludes his journey by examining the effects of light interacting with the Earth, travelling to Iceland to witness a moonbow - the band of colours released when moonbeams are refracted by the spray of a waterfall. He then visits the site of an enormous outpouring of lava to explain why hot things glow red, and reveals how the different colours of light energy from the sun transform the plains of the Serengeti in Tanzania and drive the migration of humpback whales to the Caribbean each year.(n)

programme.title: Born in China (2016)
programme.desc: Wildlife documentary telling the stories of a panda and her cub, a young golden monkey and his sister and a mother snow leopard struggling to raise her two cubs in their homeland of China. Narrated by John Krasinski.(n)

programme.title: Back in Time for the Factory
programme.desc: Over the past four weeks, 18 volunteers have experienced what life was like for the factory girls of the south Wales valleys in the 1960s, 1970s and 1980s. Now some of the women who lived through it for real talk about their lives, speaking of terrifying bosses, everyday sexism, union battles, camaraderie and of lifelong friendships forged on the production line. Presented by Alex Jones, who also reveals how the factory was brought back to life using these women's stories as inspiration.(n)

programme.title: Nadiya's British Food Adventure
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain takes a culinary road trip across Yorkshire, meeting fisherman Frank Powell near Bridlington and using some of his catch to cook baked sea trout with sun-dried tomatoes and lemons. She then heads to Huddersfield to sample local halloumi made by Syrian refugee Razan Alsous, creating watermelon and halloumi skewers. At home, Nadiya makes rhubarb and custard ice-cream sandwiches and Yorkshire puddings filled with strawberry jam and custard.(n)

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Bakeation
programme.desc: The second leg of Si King and Dave Myers' journey in search of Europe's finest baking takes them through the Low Countries. The Netherlands' heritage as a spice-trading nation shines through in such dishes as Dutch apple pie smothered in cinnamon, and Jewish buns coated in muscovado sugar. In Belgium, the duo make their own chocolate truffle cheesecake, and get busy again in Luxembourg, cooking a pork pie with Riesling jelly. Along the way they snort chocolate, taste the sweet delights of Bruges and get a recipe for macaroons with ice-cream.(n)

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The contest, hosted by Joe Lycett, reaches its final stage as the last three contestants take on a trio of celebration wear challenges before the winner is announced. Judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young start by challenging the sewers to make a little boys kilt without a pattern. In the Transformation Challenge, they each attempt to turn party dresses into fun outfits fit to wear at a carnival, before finally creating unique gowns using luxury fabric in the Made to Measure challenge. At the end of the three challenges, one of the competitors is crowned this year's winner.(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades visits the recycling centre in Altrincham in search of three pieces of skip-bound trash that he can rework and repurpose. Jay enlists lighting expert Guy Trench to give new life to an old weighing scale, while upholsterer Leigh-Anne Treadwell's creativity is pushed to the limit with a rusty wheelbarrow. Jay's own skills are tested restoring a baptismal font cover. Narrated by Arthur Smith.(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: The Downeys sample a week in Brisbane to decide whether husband Andrew's Australian dream could become a reality. But any such move would mean leaving eldest daughter Sarah behind.(n)

programme.title: Greatest Escapes to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman presents Escape to the Country highlights, focusing on older properties such as thatched cottages, timber-framed houses and mansion apartments.(n)

programme.title: Cricket: England v West Indies Greatest Matches
programme.desc: A look back at the Second Test of the 2000 series, which came from Lord's - match which saw bowlers largely come out on top.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: A collection of interesting and previously unseen finds from around the country, visiting Margam Country Park in south Wales, Highcliffe Castle in Dorset and Powderham Castle in Devon. Items range from a signed photo of Elvis to a stick-back chair, while Paul Martin explores the treasures and lavish interiors of Parham House in West Sussex.(n)

programme.title: Inside Central Station
programme.desc: Storm Hector causes havoc on the tracks, Sajid completes his last shift before he retires after 45 years in the station, and Beyonce and Jay-Z are in town.(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Road to Mexico
programme.desc: Rick's journey draws to a close as he heads east from Oaxaca to the Yucatan Peninsula, where dishes like Papadzules and Sikil Pak are enjoyed in villages across the countryside. Here, locals feast on the hottest chilli of them all, the Habanero. Rick ends his journey feasting on grilled seafood in Tulum, along the shores of the Caribbean Sea.(n)

programme.title: The Glastonbury Experience Live
programme.desc: Clara Amfo and Lauren Laverne present highlights from Glastonbury's musical vaults, on what would have been the festival's 50th anniversary. Moments include Bruce Springsteen's 2009 visit, the Arctic Monkeys' second time headlining in 2013 and Dizzee Rascal's Pyramid Stage collaboration with Florence and the Machine in 2010. Plus, Janelle Monae ruling the West Holts stage with her catchy hits in 2011 and Coldplay's debut on the Other Stage back in 2000.(n)

programme.title: Adele at Glastonbury 2016
programme.desc: As part of televised celebrations across the weekend marking what would have been 50 years of the Glastonbury Festival, tonight's programme features London-born singer-songwriter Adele's headline appearance on the world famous Pyramid Stage in June 2016, her second appearance at the festival following her small-scale debut on the Park Stage in 2007. Here, she showcased her superstar status with hits including Hello, Rumour Has It, Skyfall, Rolling in the Deep, Make You Feel and Someone Like You.(n)

programme.title: Coldplay at Glastonbury 2016
programme.desc: Coldplay closed Glastonbury 2016 with a bang, bringing their powerful and emotive pop anthems to the Pyramid Stage. Playing hits including Fix You, Paradise and Yellow, they were also joined onstage by Barry Gibb for renditions of Bee Gees classics To Love Somebody and Stayin' Alive. The band also paid tribute to Viola Beach, a young rock band (discovered via BBC Music Introducing) whose members and manager died in a car accident in Sweden just a few months earlier.(n)

programme.title: Books That Made Britain
programme.desc: Chris Packham takes a tour of the Cornish coast and reveals the stories behind five classic books set in the county, including Winston Graham's Poldark and Daphe du Maurier's Frenchman's Creek.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Another half hour in the company of American artist Bob Ross as he puts paint to canvas. Today he takes viewers on a summertime nature walk ending at a secluded lake.(n)

programme.title: Tess of the D'Urbervilles
programme.desc: Tess's baby Sorrow dies after falling seriously ill and she is forced to bury him alone when Parson Tringham refuses. She then leaves home and finds work at a dairy farm, where she falls in love with Angel Clare and agrees to marry him. However, the bride-to-be suffers an attack of conscience the night before her wedding and decides to tell her prospective husband about her past. Gemma Arterton stars.(n)

programme.title: Boy George's 1970s: Save Me from Suburbia
programme.desc: The BBC's My Generation season continues as Boy George revisits his teenage years. While history may remember the 1970s as being marked by strikes, poverty and civil discord, the singer reveals that - for the young George O'Dowd - the decade was the beginning of his musical, social and sexual coming-of-age. He discusses how sexual repression and disenfranchisement, along with the advent of glam rock, disco and punk, gave rise to the flamboyant persona he would later unleash on the world, via Culture Club's memorable first appearance on Top of the Pops in 1982.(n)

programme.title: Glastonbury (2006)
programme.desc: Julien Temple's documentary about the world-famous music festival, which began in 1970 and continues to attract a diverse and enthusiastic range of revellers. The film features performances from artists including Morrissey, David Bowie, Bjork, James Brown, Nick Cave, Joe Strummer, Billy Bragg and Blur, while amateur footage provides an authentic taste of the event.(n)

programme.title: Art of Persia
programme.desc: Broadcaster and journalist Samira Ahmed reveals how Iran preserved its distinctive language and culture despite the Arab conquest of Persia.(n)

programme.title: Books That Made Britain
programme.desc: Chris Packham takes a tour of the Cornish coast and reveals the stories behind five classic books set in the county, including Winston Graham's Poldark and Daphe du Maurier's Frenchman's Creek.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Another half hour in the company of American artist Bob Ross as he puts paint to canvas. Today he takes viewers on a summertime nature walk ending at a secluded lake.(n)

programme.title: Boy George's 1970s: Save Me from Suburbia
programme.desc: The BBC's My Generation season continues as Boy George revisits his teenage years. While history may remember the 1970s as being marked by strikes, poverty and civil discord, the singer reveals that - for the young George O'Dowd - the decade was the beginning of his musical, social and sexual coming-of-age. He discusses how sexual repression and disenfranchisement, along with the advent of glam rock, disco and punk, gave rise to the flamboyant persona he would later unleash on the world, via Culture Club's memorable first appearance on Top of the Pops in 1982.(n)

programme.title: Opera Italia
programme.desc: Antonio Pappano traces Verdi's role in Italian history and culture, travelling to Le Roncole to learn about the composer's childhood amid political turmoil, and discovering the effect this had on his operas in later life. He examines six of Verdi's most famous works, rehearses La Traviata at London's Royal Opera House, and conducts Va Pensiero from Nabucco at an open-air concert in Naples.(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell hosts the edition first shown on November 9, featuring performances by Janet Jackson, Electribe 101, Lisa Stansfield, Aaron Neville and Linda Ronstadt, and the Wonder Stuff.(n)

programme.title: Kermode and Mayo's Home Entertainment Service
programme.desc: Mark Kermode and Simon Mayo present a guide to Netflix's The Eddy, BBC's Normal People, Eliza Hittman-directed Never Rarely Sometimes Always, Mubi docu-feature Romantic Comedy and Terry Gilliam's classic Twelve Monkeys. Featuring a chat with director Lenny Abrahamson.(n)

programme.title: Elbow at Glastonbury 2011
programme.desc: Coverage of a performance on the Pyramid Stage in the year of the release of their fifth album Build a Rocket Boys! and still flush with the Mercury Prize-winning success of The Seldom Seen Kid. Featuring songs including Open Arms, Grounds for Divorce, Neat Little Rows and One Day Like This.(n)

programme.title: Arthur Lee & Love: Forever Changes at Glastonbury 2003
programme.desc: A set from the 2003 Glastonbury festival by Love with the late Arthur Lee, complete with full backing band and string section, featuring tracks from the influential 1967 album Forever Changes, including performances of Alone Again Or, Between Clark and Hilldale, Andmoreagain and You Set the Scene.(n)

programme.title: Glastonbury Backstage Acoustics
programme.desc: The first of three compilations of the many impromptu, stripped-down performances that have graced the BBC Two presentation tent since coverage began back in 1997. Pop stars, legendary acts and breakthrough artists have all been featured over the years, giving audiences the chance to see artists from stages the cameras don't always cover.(n)

programme.title: Jay-Z at Glastonbury 2008
programme.desc: Another chance to see rapper Jay-Z's legendary performance from the Pyramid Stage. Opening with a tongue-in-cheek performance of Wonderwall - following criticism of his booking by Noel Gallagher - he tore the house down with a set packed full of his best-loved hits, included 99 Problems and Show Me What You Got.(n)

programme.title: The Summer of Love: How Hippies Changed the World
programme.desc: The first of a two-part documentary recounting the 1967 coming-together that would change the world. The film traces the roots of the hippies to a 19th-century German sect of wandering naturalists that brought its freethinking ideas to California after the Second World War. There it merged with people expressing an interest in Eastern mystical concepts of human nature expounded by thinkers like Aleister Crowley and Aldous Huxley. Added to the mix was the CIA-developed drug LSD and a wave of student activists and anti-war protestors. The outcome was San Francisco's Summer of Love.(n)

programme.title: Country Music by Ken Burns
programme.desc: The history of the musical art form from 1973 to 1983, when Dolly Parton crossed over to mainstream success and became the most famous woman in country music. George Jones and Tammy Wynette seemingly lived out their songs' tragic lyrics, while Hank Williams Jr. emerged from his father's shadow and debuted on the Opry show at just 11 years old. Contributors include Dolly Parton, Kris Kristofferson, Emmylou Harris, Willie Nelson, Brenda Lee and Hank Williams Jr. Narrated by Peter Coyote.(n)

programme.title: Opera Italia
programme.desc: Antonio Pappano traces Verdi's role in Italian history and culture, travelling to Le Roncole to learn about the composer's childhood amid political turmoil, and discovering the effect this had on his operas in later life. He examines six of Verdi's most famous works, rehearses La Traviata at London's Royal Opera House, and conducts Va Pensiero from Nabucco at an open-air concert in Naples.(n)

programme.title: Glastonbury Backstage Acoustics

programme.title: Toots & The Maytals at Glastonbury 2010
programme.desc: The ska group's performance on the West Holts Stage, featuring songs such as Pressure Drop, Louie Louie, Monkey Man and 54-46 Was My Number. Introduced by Mark Radcliffe.(n)

programme.title: The Young Montalbano
programme.desc: The detective attempts to navigate local gossip in Vigata when posters appear around the town questioning the moral status of one of its female inhabitants. However, when a murder takes place, the resulting investigation unearths some unsavoury facts about the victim. Drama, starring Michele Riondino. In Italian.(n)

programme.title: The Chemical Brothers at Glastonbury 2000
programme.desc: The dance duo's Friday night headline performance on the Pyramid Stage at the iconic festival. Featuring hits including Hey Boy Hey Girl, Block Rockin' Beats and Setting Sun.(n)

programme.title: Best of Glastonbury 2000
programme.desc: Highlights of the summer festival, which saw thousands of music fans swarm to the legendary site to see acts including David Bowie, Travis, Pet Shop Boys and Nine Inch Nails.(n)

programme.title: Football, Prince William and Our Mental Health
programme.desc: Documentary following the Duke of Cambridge's work to break the taboo surrounding mental health, using football as a way to encourage men to open up and talk about their emotions. The documentary follows the Duke as he meets players and fans from grassroots to the elite of the game, and openly discusses their mental health challenges. Former England goalkeeper Joe Hart talks about how he has learnt to cope with difficulties at the very top of the game, and William hears from a group of bereaved fathers who use their local football team as a support network and safe space to talk. Former Premier League footballer Marvin Sordell opens up about his struggles with depression, while Chelsea manager Frank Lampard compares life now with his early experiences of professional football.(n)

However, they face another tough task against the Red Devils, who are yet to concede a goal in the competition this term. With analysis from Alan Shearer and Ian Wright.(n)

programme.title: Thierry Henry: My France, My Euros
programme.desc: The former France striker and Arsenal's all-time leading scorer looks at European Championship stories, assessing the social and cultural impact football has across the continent. As Henry's home country prepares to host Euro 2016 following last November's terror attacks in Paris, he explores how the beautiful game can unify a nation, from the success of the Netherlands in 1988 to Euro 96 in England, when 'football came home'. Contributors include Cesc Fabregas, Peter Schmeichel, Ruud Gullit, Jurgen Klinsmann, Alan Shearer, David Baddiel and Ian Broudie.(n)

The next day his body is found in a locked room and Father Brown suspects he has been poisoned. Such events prompt local speculation to mount that Kembleford is under threat of an alien invasion. Mark Williams stars.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah is on a mission to help find a country home for £300,000 in the East Midlands for an eager buyer who has brought along her friendly neighbour for support. While in the region, Sonali pays a visit to an Edwardian house in Worksop, now managed by the National Trust, and steps back in time to learn about everyday life and work in the region during that era.(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers visit Hook, Hampshire to help young couple Cat and Joel. The newlyweds dream of a family garden for the children that they plan to have - a space full of grass and wildlife friendly flowers. However, all they have now is a concrete garden made up of nothing but paving stones.(n)

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: The dealers compete to buy an ornate clock by the distinguished British horologist Charles Frodsham, an antique tombola machine, a 19th-century armchair and a vintage coffee set.(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman.(n)

programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Reporting Scotland; Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Chris Ramsey host the magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest.(n)

programme.title: Alan Bennett's Talking Heads
programme.desc: Harriet Walter stars in this updated version of Alan Bennett's dramatic monologue Soldiering On, which originally starred Stephanie Cole. After the death of her husband, Muriel is left looking after her mentally ill daughter. She's a strong woman and always has been. But when her son Giles turns up with papers for her to sign, it turns out she is quite a rich widow. But can he be trusted?(n)

programme.title: You Are What You Wear
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal hosts the fashion makeover show, with volunteers including an Essex pawnbroker who has put off his wedding three times because he has lost confidence in his looks and a biker chick from Newport Pagnell, who rocks up wanting a funked-up feminine look. The experts also get their hands on a stay-at-home mum who wants her fashion mojo back after battling a brain condition and a lifelong military man who would like a smart casual look perfect for a single gentleman in his sixties. Plus, advice for viewers on what jeans work best for different body shapes.(n)

programme.title: Make Me Famous
programme.desc: One-off drama about the impact of fame on reality TV stars. One year ago, Billy thought his life was set to change for ever after appearing on a constructed reality show. But while some of his co-stars' careers are thriving, a series of tabloid kiss-and-tells force Billy's vulnerabilities to the surface. Can he escape the reputation that now precedes him? Starring Tom Brittney, Amanda Abbington and Aiysha Hart. Written by presenter and DJ Reggie Yates.(n)

programme.title: The One Show

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Dougie Vipond and Nick Nairn are in their kitchens cooking with Scottish produce. Euan McIlwraith reflects on how the bird of prey population has changed over the time he's been reporting and Arlene Stuart investigates the impact of Covid-19 on the traditional Common Riding Festivals.(n)

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Ben discovers Nick is taking Viagra, but worse is to come when Susan suggests he should try it as well - an idea that ends in a trip to casualty and further embarrassment at yoga classes. Domestic comedy, starring Robert Lindsay and Zoe Wanamaker.(n)

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lucy takes the kids away to her parents while Lee stays in the house to oversee the building work being done their kitchen, but unfortunately the tradesman he has employed has an annoyingly relaxed attitude to deadlines. Comedy, starring Lee Mack and Sally Bretton.(n)

programme.title: The Other One
programme.desc: Holly Walsh and Pippa Brown's comedy about two sisters, both called Catherine Walcott, who have no idea the other exists until the point following the death of their father continues. Cathy proposes they get late, lamented dad Colin a memorial bench but, as Cat doesn't have the money, she has to improvise. Cathy also agrees to teach Cat how to ride a bike - though the results prove disastrous leading to her seeking emergency dental appointment. Ellie White and Lauren Socha play the unlikely siblings, alongside co-stars Siobhan Finneran and Amit Shah.(n)

programme.title: Peter Kay's Car Share
programme.desc: After moving in with her sister, Kayleigh is now travelling to work on her own, but will she manage to resist temptation or call her old car share friend, John? Comedy about two supermarket employees, starring Peter Kay and Sian Gibson.(n)

programme.title: Pretty Single
programme.desc: Law graduate Michaela spends hours perfecting her look for photos on social media. It remains to be seen, therefore, whether going on a series of dates completely free of make-up will successfully pave the way to her perfect match.(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Former Sweden defender Jonas Olsson, world champion swimmer Fran Halsall, darts star Fallon Sherrock and Great Britain sprinter Harry Aikines-Aryeetey join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz.(n)

programme.title: The Lady in the Van (2015)
programme.desc: The true story of writer Alan Bennett's friendship with an eccentric homeless woman, whom he befriended in the 1970s before allowing her to park her dilapidated Bedford van outside his Camden home, where she ended up staying for 15 years. Comedy-drama, starring Maggie Smith, Alex Jennings, Roger Allam and Jim Broadbent.(n)

programme.desc: Germany v England. Another chance to see the Euro 96 semi-final from Wembley Stadium.(n)

programme.title: Football, Prince William and Our Mental Health
programme.desc: Documentary following the Duke of Cambridge's work to break the taboo surrounding mental health, using football as a way to encourage men to open up and talk about their emotions. The documentary follows the Duke as he meets players and fans from grassroots to the elite of the game, and openly discusses their mental health challenges. Former England goalkeeper Joe Hart talks about how he has learnt to cope with difficulties at the very top of the game, and William hears from a group of bereaved fathers who use their local football team as a support network and safe space to talk. Former Premier League footballer Marvin Sordell opens up about his struggles with depression, while Chelsea manager Frank Lampard compares life now with his early experiences of professional football.(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Norwich City v Manchester United (Kick-off 5.30pm). Gary Lineker presents all the action from the sixth-round tie at Carrow Road. Norwich are struggling in the Premier League this season, but produced one of their best performances of the campaign to defeat Tottenham Hotspur on penalties away from home in the previous round. However, they face another tough task against the Red Devils, who are yet to concede a goal in the competition this term. With analysis from Alan Shearer and Ian Wright.(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a 1990s edition of the celebrity quiz, featuring teams of famous faces from the decade seeking unlikely answers. The contestants are Jayne Middlemiss, Diane Louise Jordan, Sarah Cawood, Jeremy Spake, Angus Loughran, Kriss Akabusi MBE, Philip Franks and Sue Jenkins.(n)

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's Big Laughs
programme.desc: The comedian looks back at some of the best moments from the Michael McIntyre's Big Show series, revisiting some of the biggest laughs from the past five seasons. Tonight, he looks back on the hilarious Send To All's from singer Olly Murs and EastEnders' Danny Dyer, as well as the moment singer Peter Andre was awoken by Michael in the middle of the night to play the Midnight Gameshow.(n)

programme.title: Peter Crouch: Save Our Summer
programme.desc: The former footballer hosts a new variety entertainment show, featuring live music, comedy skits and a host of celebrity guests via video links. Peter will also be joined in each episode by TV and BBC Radio 1 presenter Maya Jama, as well as Taskmaster's Alex Horne and his comedy band the Horne Section. This time, Jimmy Carr and Melanie C pop by for a chat and a game of Musical VAR, and Jessica Ennis-Hill takes on Max Whitlock in The Sporting Fix. Plus, a performance from the Black Eyed Peas in The Garden Sessions.(n)

programme.title: Thierry Henry: My France, My Euros
programme.desc: The former France striker and Arsenal's all-time leading scorer looks at European Championship stories, assessing the social and cultural impact football has across the continent. As Henry's home country prepares to host Euro 2016 following last November's terror attacks in Paris, he explores how the beautiful game can unify a nation, from the success of the Netherlands in 1988 to Euro 96 in England, when 'football came home'. Contributors include Cesc Fabregas, Peter Schmeichel, Ruud Gullit, Jurgen Klinsmann, Alan Shearer, David Baddiel and Ian Broudie.(n)

programme.title: McFarland, USA (2015)
programme.desc: A former American football coach takes a job at an underprivileged school with a mostly Hispanic student body, and finds he has a lot to learn about their lives. When he discovers many of his pupils have a talent for running, he forms an athletics team and leads them to glory. Fact-based drama, starring Kevin Costner and Ramiro Rodriguez.(n)

The contestants are Jayne Middlemiss, Diane Louise Jordan, Sarah Cawood, Jeremy Spake, Angus Loughran, Kriss Akabusi MBE, Philip Franks and Sue Jenkins.(n)

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's Big Laughs
programme.desc: The comedian looks back at some of the best moments from the Michael McIntyre's Big Show series, revisiting some of the biggest laughs from the past five seasons. Tonight, he looks back on the hilarious Send To All's from singer Olly Murs and EastEnders' Danny Dyer, as well as the moment singer Peter Andre was awoken by Michael in the middle of the night to play the Midnight Gameshow.(n)

programme.title: Peter Crouch: Save Our Summer
programme.desc: The former footballer hosts a new variety entertainment show, featuring live music, comedy skits and a host of celebrity guests via video links. Peter will also be joined in each episode by TV and BBC Radio 1 presenter Maya Jama, as well as Taskmaster's Alex Horne and his comedy band the Horne Section. This time, Jimmy Carr and Melanie C pop by for a chat and a game of Musical VAR, and Jessica Ennis-Hill takes on Max Whitlock in The Sporting Fix. Plus, a performance from the Black Eyed Peas in The Garden Sessions.(n)

programme.title: Thierry Henry: My France, My Euros
programme.desc: The former France striker and Arsenal's all-time leading scorer looks at European Championship stories, assessing the social and cultural impact football has across the continent. As Henry's home country prepares to host Euro 2016 following last November's terror attacks in Paris, he explores how the beautiful game can unify a nation, from the success of the Netherlands in 1988 to Euro 96 in England, when 'football came home'. Contributors include Cesc Fabregas, Peter Schmeichel, Ruud Gullit, Jurgen Klinsmann, Alan Shearer, David Baddiel and Ian Broudie.(n)

programme.title: McFarland, USA (2015)
programme.desc: A former American football coach takes a job at an underprivileged school with a mostly Hispanic student body, and finds he has a lot to learn about their lives. When he discovers many of his pupils have a talent for running, he forms an athletics team and leads them to glory. Fact-based drama, starring Kevin Costner and Ramiro Rodriguez.(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Daniel De Simone investigates a global network of neo-Nazis that aims to destroy society, discovering that it is recruiting in the UK. Police say right-wing extremism is the fastest growing terrorist threat in the UK and that the coronavirus pandemic may be leaving young people vulnerable to radicalisation. As Daniel investigates he notices certain names cropping up again and again and tracks down some of the movement's most influential figures and reveals how the network operates across the globe.(n)

programme.title: Lombardy: Journey to Italy's Virus Heart
programme.desc: The northern Italian region of Lombardy saw the first major Coronavirus outbreak in Europe. programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates another comical selection filmed by viewers, featuring a car crushed by a mudslide, a toddler terrified by a chicken and a dog peeing into a smartphone. Plus, a young Peaky Blinder runs amok at a wedding.(n)

programme.title: Ross Kemp Living With
programme.desc: An investigation into the problem of addiction to opioids - opium-derived drugs - which are used in powerful prescription painkillers. Ross meets a woman who ended up with a serious addiction after being prescribed drugs for her bad back. He also learns about fentanyl, a painkiller used in cancer treatment, which can be much stronger than heroin and is being advertised for sale illegally in the UK. Ross comes face to face with a drug dealer who has started cutting fentanyl into his product and meets a former chemist whose daughter died from an overdose after taking heroin laced with the drug.(n)

programme.title: Britain's Brightest Celebrity Family
programme.desc: Two returning families compete in the first semi-final of the knockout tournament, bringing them one step closer to the chance to win £25,000 for their chosen charity and the coveted title of Britain's Brightest Celebrity Family. The Chase's Anne Hegerty, aka The Governess, asks the questions.(n)

programme.title: The Martin Lewis Money Show: Live
programme.desc: The financial journalist presents more advice for people who have found themselves living with financial uncertainty. Martin answers viewers' questions on how the economic crisis affects family finances, jobs and consumer rights.(n)

programme.title: Unbelievable Moments Caught on Camera
programme.desc: A couple are caught on camera during their first date as one of them suffers a heart attack, two antique dealers make a death-defying escape when their Maryland shop fills with flood water and a huge cruise ship is seen crashing into a small leisure boat in Venice. Plus, the touching moment that a baby hears her big sister for the first time and an incredible farewell flashmob for a much-loved headteacher in Greater Manchester.(n)

programme.title: Best Walks with a View with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: At 694 metres in height, Pen-y-ghent is the smallest fell on the Yorkshire Three Peaks Challenge, but the dramatic summit affords far-reaching views above the Dales, with plenty of surprises en route. The peak lies 3km east of Horton in Ribblesdale and the Pennine Way links the summit to the village. As Julia reveals, the route is around 5km in length, as the Way curves initially to the north before turning east to reach the summit.(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Leanne is shocked that Steve has booked a family trip to Cornwall, and David is stunned when Shona leans in for a kiss and suggests they have sex. Toyah joins Evelyn and Nina on their protest, while Seb and Alina agree to meet for a drink as mates.(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street Icons: Audrey Roberts - 40 Fabulous Years
programme.desc: A celebration of Sue Nicholls' time on Coronation Street, which began in April 1979. The actress relives some of Audrey's quirky highlights and most dramatic lows, while Tracy Shaw and Nikki Sanderson - who played salon girls Maxine and Candice - return to the Street to surprise Sue. Brian Capron also reminisces about their dramatic scenes together during the storyline involving his serial killer character Richard Hillman. Narrated by Sally Lindsay.(n)

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective investigates the mysterious death of businessman John Shearwood, found floating in the water under the Gateshead Millennium Bridge when he was supposed to be on a golfing trip to Ireland. The man's wife Stella and brother Luke are baffled to discover he was working as an informant for HM Revenue and Customs. Chief Revenue investigator Owen Preece (guest star Robert Glenister) reveals John's business manager Mark Donovan has been running an alcohol scam through their haulage firm and the deceased had agreed to offer information in exchange for immunity from prosecution. Was his cover blown? Owen doesn't think so, and Mark has an alibi - unlike John's wife. Brenda Blethyn stars, with David Leon as her trusty sidekick Joe.(n)

programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents.(n)

programme.desc: An interview with skaters Jayne Torvill and Christopher Dean, who open up about the challenges they have faced in life, including Christopher's separation from his mother at a young age and Jayne's struggle to start a family, which led her to adopt two children with her husband. The Olympic ice-dancing gold medallists also answer questions about whether their relationship has ever been more than professional.(n)

programme.desc: Comedy drama following the adventures of an Australian family who move to an isolated seaside village in western Ireland, with the parents having a secret to hide.(n)

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: The TV chef returns for the weekend chat show, joined at home by fellow chef Atul Kochhar and celebrity guests Rick Astley and Craig Revel Horwood, who gets a crash course in sausage-making. programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner prepare a feast of Asian food, beginning with prawn satay served with smashed cucumber. John makes a Chinese-style pot roast chicken, while Lisa prepares her favourite Thai curry with a lychee granita for dessert.(n)

programme.title: James Martin's American Adventure
programme.desc: The TV chef continues his American adventure in Austin, Texas. James starts with a breakfast taco at iconic Torchy's, followed by a cowboy themed shopping spree. He then cooks in front of a mural dedicated to his favourite ingredient - butter.(n)

programme.title: The Masked Singer US
programme.desc: More mystery celebrities battle it out in a singing contest while dressed head to toe in elaborate costumes, while a panel of more famous faces tries to guess their identities. Nick Cannon oversees the contest, as mystery singers dressed as Flamingo, Leopard, Black Widow, Skeleton, Thingamajig and Butterfly take to the stage, while panellists Robin Thicke, Jenny McCarthy, Nicole Scherzinger, and regular star of the British spin-off Ken Jeong try to work out who is behind the mask.(n)

programme.title: Ninja Warrior UK
programme.desc: Celebrity contestants including Gethin Jones, Jenni Falconer and Josh Cuthbert test their strength, speed and courage in this obstacle-course challenge, hosted by Ben Shephard, Rochelle Humes and Chris Kamara. Those who make it to the end of the adrenaline-fuelled mission face one final task - scaling the heights of a tower named Mount Midoriyama - as they battle it out for the title of Ninja Warrior UK.(n)

programme.title: Alan Carr's Epic Gameshow
programme.desc: The comedian continues his run of classic quiz revivals with another game of Play Your Cards Right. Four plucky couples test their luck against the ever-unpredictable deck of cards as they work their way higher and lower through the game, for the chance to win a £30,000 jackpot - but success can rest on the turn of a single card.(n)

programme.title: Spectre (2015)
programme.desc: James Bond goes rogue after receiving a cryptic message that sets him on the trail of a secretive criminal network. While his colleagues face a new threat closer to home, Bond embarks on a globetrotting journey in search of the elusive mastermind behind the syndicate - whom he discovers may have links to his own past and had involvement in previous events during some of Bond's most dangerous missions. Spy thriller, starring Daniel Craig, Christoph Waltz, Lea Seydoux, Ben Whishaw and Ralph Fiennes.(n)

programme.title: Ben 10
programme.desc: Our hero must be creative if he is to keep up with his foes!(n)

programme.title: Drop Dead Weird

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen

programme.title: James Martin's American Adventure
programme.desc: The TV chef continues his American adventure in Austin, Texas. James starts with a breakfast taco at iconic Torchy's, followed by a cowboy themed shopping spree. He then cooks in front of a mural dedicated to his favourite ingredient - butter.(n)

Plus, three races from Newmarket, including the 2.45 Criterion Stakes. With analysis from Jason Weaver, reports by Luke Harvey, and commentary from Richard Hoiles.(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot.(n)

programme.title: The Masked Singer US
programme.desc: More mystery celebrities battle it out in a singing contest while dressed head to toe in elaborate costumes, while a panel of more famous faces tries to guess their identities. Nick Cannon oversees the contest, as mystery singers dressed as Flamingo, Leopard, Black Widow, Skeleton, Thingamajig and Butterfly take to the stage, while panellists Robin Thicke, Jenny McCarthy, Nicole Scherzinger, and regular star of the British spin-off Ken Jeong try to work out who is behind the mask.(n)

programme.title: Ninja Warrior UK
programme.desc: Celebrity contestants including Gethin Jones, Jenni Falconer and Josh Cuthbert test their strength, speed and courage in this obstacle-course challenge, hosted by Ben Shephard, Rochelle Humes and Chris Kamara. Those who make it to the end of the adrenaline-fuelled mission face one final task - scaling the heights of a tower named Mount Midoriyama - as they battle it out for the title of Ninja Warrior UK.(n)

programme.title: Alan Carr's Epic Gameshow
programme.desc: The comedian continues his run of classic quiz revivals with another game of Play Your Cards Right. programme.title: Spectre (2015)
programme.desc: James Bond goes rogue after receiving a cryptic message that sets him on the trail of a secretive criminal network. While his colleagues face a new threat closer to home, Bond embarks on a globetrotting journey in search of the elusive mastermind behind the syndicate - whom he discovers may have links to his own past and had involvement in previous events during some of Bond's most dangerous missions. Spy thriller, starring Daniel Craig, Christoph Waltz, Lea Seydoux, Ben Whishaw and Ralph Fiennes.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Local boy DS Ben Jones is a mine of information for his boss DCI Barnaby when a reclusive elderly couple are murdered in their home and a spate of killings appears to be linked to both a fatal road accident and the disappearance of a teenager 20 years earlier. A bitter struggle over plans to demolish homes in a road development ensues, and wild pronouncements from the local psychic ensure tensions in the village of Dunstan remain at fever pitch. Maggie Steed and Ben Crompton guest star.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth investigates the murder of her old friend Father Gorman, using a list of names sent by the priest moments before his death to bring the guilty party to justice. programme.desc: Coverage of day six from Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, featuring the opening session of a semi-final, played over the best of 17 frames. programme.title: Snooker: Tour Championship Live
programme.desc: Further coverage of day six from Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, featuring the concluding session of a semi-final, played over the best of 17 frames. Neil Robertson and Ronnie O'Sullivan were the victorious semi-finalists last year, overcoming Mark Allen and Judd Trump respectively to set up a repeat of the Players Championship final two weeks earlier. The Rocket would emerge victorious in the showpiece match, eventually triumphing by a scoreline of 13-11, including two century breaks and 10 runs of 50 or more. Jill Douglas presents, with analysis from Stephen Hendry, Alan McManus and Neal Foulds, and commentary by Clive Everton and Phil Yates.(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur attempts to protect his friend Sidney, who on mistakenly being diagnosed with a fatal illness, arranged for a hitman to kill him so his wife would benefit from his life insurance. While Ray attempts to counter the deal, the best safe house the dodgy dealer can provide is a leaky caravan in Brighton, the property of Dave's sister. Andrew Sachs guest stars, with George Cole and Gary Webster.(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle begin an investigation into why a city's crime rate is steadily falling. They later find themselves victimised by local officers after being charged with a minor traffic offence. Drama, starring Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins.(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Regan's holiday is disrupted by the escape of a violent psychopath from prison. Tim Cook, dubbed 'The Leopard' by the press has vowed murderous revenge on the detective inspector for his earlier capture, and has friends on the outside prepared to harbour him while he plans his attack. Crime drama, starring John Thaw and Dennis Waterman.(n)

programme.title: Snooker: Tour Championship Live
programme.desc: Coverage of day six from Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, featuring the opening session of a semi-final, played over the best of 17 frames. Judd Trump, Shaun Murphy, Mark Selby and Neil Robertson came into this tournament as the top four seeds, and they will have expected to still be involved as the conclusion draws closer. Jill Douglas presents, with analysis from Stephen Hendry, Alan McManus and Neal Foulds, and commentary by Clive Everton and Phil Yates.(n)

programme.title: Fishing Allstars
programme.desc: At the Willinghurst fishery complex near Guildford, Adam Rooney and Dean Macey start off on one of the easier lakes where Adam is soon bagging up chunky carp on his match gear.(n)

programme.title: Mr Bean
programme.desc: Rowan Atkinson slips into silly mode once more as his comic alter ego visits the launderette and somehow manages to lose his trousers, getting himself into a real pickle as he tries to retrieve them. programme.title: Mr Bean

programme.title: World of Sport

programme.title: Snooker: Tour Championship Live
programme.desc: Further coverage of day six from Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, featuring the concluding session of a semi-final, played over the best of 17 frames. Neil Robertson and Ronnie O'Sullivan were the victorious semi-finalists last year, overcoming Mark Allen and Judd Trump respectively to set up a repeat of the Players Championship final two weeks earlier. The Rocket would emerge victorious in the showpiece match, eventually triumphing by a scoreline of 13-11, including two century breaks and 10 runs of 50 or more. Jill Douglas presents, with analysis from Stephen Hendry, Alan McManus and Neal Foulds, and commentary by Clive Everton and Phil Yates.(n)

programme.title: The Nice Guys (2016)
programme.desc: A private detective investigates the murder of a porn star in 1970s Los Angeles, only to run into a hired thug employed to scare him off. Crime comedy, starring Russell Crowe and Ryan Gosling.(n)

To begin with the students are split into different work groups according to their skin colour. With a £100,000 budget, the pair have decided to do a lot of the building themselves, and Kevin is on hand to discover whether they're up to the challenge.(n)

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Ari refuses to believe that brother Tane is a reformed character. Ziggy is in shock as his parents begin the process of dividing their assets.(n)

programme.desc: A round-up of the evening's headlines, including coverage of the latest national and international stories.(n)

programme.title: A Year in the Wild
programme.desc: Unseasonably cold summer weather causes difficulties for several animals in the Yorkshire Dales and North York Moors, including an injured roe deer and its offspring. High up in Malham Cove, two 40-day-old peregrines must learn how to fly, while south of the North York Moors National Park, a pair of grebes carry their youngsters around on their backs.(n)

programme.title: Warship: Life Below Deck
programme.desc: When HMS Duncan is given a mission to help defeat the last ISIS forces in Iraq and Syria, the crew need to focus in order to achieve their goal.(n)

programme.title: Building Britain's Canals
programme.desc: Writer and historian Dan Jones navigates Britain's longest canal to be built by just one company, the Leeds and Liverpool Canal. Built to link the economically booming counties of Yorkshire and Lancashire, this 19th-century superhighway was experimental for the time, and made Liverpool the busiest port in the world. In Burnley, the cotton capital of the 19th century, Dan visits the world's only steam-powered cotton mill, where enough cloth was woven each year to reach to the moon and back two-and-a-half times.(n)

programme.title: Inside the World's Greatest Hotels
programme.desc: A look behind the scenes at the Dorchester in London's affluent area of Mayfair, which has been associated with the rich and famous since it first opened in 1931. A royal stag party is just one of the subjects covered in the programme, along with fascinating accounts of engagements, honeymoons and an untimely death.(n)

programme.title: The World's Most Luxurious Private Jets
programme.desc: Luxury travel blogger Em Sheldon catches a flight on one of the most extravagant private jets in the world, which cost more than £80m to build and kit out. The cabin of this jet was designed by leading international designer Edese Doret, who was famously asked to create designs for the US President's aircraft Air Force One, and the blinds, music and cabin temperature can be controlled from iPads tucked into each seat.(n)

programme.title: Eamonn & Ruth: How the Other Half Lives
programme.desc: Eamonn Holmes and Ruth Langsford return to explore the extravagant lifestyles of the super-rich, beginning by heading to the largest city in the United Arab Emirates. After experiencing a luxury night under the stars in the desert, they visit the Burj Khalifa - the tallest building in the world - to meet a multimillionaire who calls it home.(n)

programme.title: The 21.co.uk Live Casino Show
programme.desc: Interactive gambling.(n)

programme.title: A Greek Odyssey with Bettany Hughes
programme.desc: Bettany visits the sacred isle of Delos, where it has been forbidden for anyone to permanently reside since ancient times. Wandering the ruins, Bettany discovers a dark history to this incredible site as she explores how the Romans turned Delos into a marketplace for their slave trade. She then moves on to Ikaria, an island famous for its long-lived population, and gets a lesson in wine-making. As she moves on to Mykonos, Bettany's boat is caught in a storm reaching a 10 on the Beaufort scale.(n)

programme.title: Cruising with Jane McDonald
programme.desc: The singer heads to Alaska, America's largest state and home to some of the most breathtaking scenery on the planet. She takes a helicopter trip up to an ice field for a dog-sledding adventure, and later witnesses the majesty of the Margery glacier, a 21-mile stretch of ice in the Glacier Bay National Park. On board Holland America's ship Nieuw Amsterdam, Jane goes backstage to meet the cast of the nightly musicals, has a cooking lesson from the executive chef and reveals how to make the most of a lazy day in port.(n)

programme.title: House Doctor
programme.desc: Ann Maurice and Alistair Appleton visit Scraptoft in Leicester, where a five-bedroom house valued at £229,500 needs a makeover to enhance its appeal. programme.title: The Gadget Show
programme.desc: Jon Bentley road-tests one the hottest smartphones on the market, the Samsung Galaxy S20, and reveals whether it's worth its premium price tag. Georgie Barrat gets a behind-the-scenes tour of Las Vegas, finding out how Sin City is reinventing itself in to one of the most technologically advanced places in the world. Plus, Ortis Deley reveals how 1995 defined the tech world of today, while a new feature sees Craig Charles attempt to turn a house into a smart home using so-called 'easy fit' DIY kits.(n)

programme.title: The World's Most Scenic Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Bill Nighy narrates a trip through Wales' highest peaks and deepest valleys. Starting in Pwllheli in the north west, the route heads along the lines that were built during the slate industry's heyday. At Porthmadog, the journey briefly switches to the reconstructed steam trains of the Welsh Highland National Heritage Railway to Minffordd station. Nearby is the village of Portmeirion, famous for its role in the classic TV series The Prisoner. Rejoining the Cambrian Line, local farmers reveal what the railway means to them, before the route takes in the seaside resort of Aberdovey.(n)

programme.title: A Greek Odyssey with Bettany Hughes
programme.desc: The historian and classicist goes Greek island-hopping to explore their history from the time of the Ancients to the present day. In Santorini, she visits a Minoan city preserved in time by one of the most violent volcanic eruptions in history. Bettany's next port of call is Naxos, where in an abandoned marble quarry, she finds a genuinely monumental statue of the god that has lain there for thousands of years, before heading to a taverna to take part in some local festivities. The presenter later arrives in Sifnos, where long-distance communications were mastered millennia before telegrams or telephones.(n)

programme.title: When... Goes Horribly Wrong
programme.desc: Sarah Greene narrates a compilation of infamous moments from television history when programmes went disastrously wrong. Among the incidents featured are the death of Tommy Cooper in mid-performance, an invasion of the BBC news studios, and the wrong Miss Universe being crowned in front of 200 million viewers worldwide. Anthea Turner shares her memories of nearly being blown up when a pyrotechnic effect went wrong.(n)
