programme.desc: Ex-lawyer florist Abby delivers black roses at college, but returning minutes later, she finds an old rival murdered. Mystery sequel, starring Brooke Shields and Brennan Elliott.(n)

programme.title: Columbo: Murder Under Glass (1978)
programme.desc: The crumpled detective applies his well-honed instincts to a case of blackmail and murder in the world of haute cuisine when a restaurant owner is found dead after throwing a renowned food critic off his premises. Crime drama from The Silence of the Lambs director Jonathan Demme, starring Peter Falk, Louis Jourdan, Shera Danese and Richard Dysart.(n)

programme.title: The Thames: Britain's Great River with Tony Robinson
programme.desc: Reaching central London, the actor boards an industrial working ship on the Thames, learning it is a route not without risks as ships must squeeze under bridges at high tide and avoid running aground at low tide, before getting a bird's-eye view of the river from the Shard. Tony then visits the East End of London, reflecting on his teenage years working on the docks, and ends his journey in the Thames Estuary.(n)

programme.title: Great British Gardens: Season by Season with Carol Klein
programme.desc: Carol Klein visits spectacular gardens across the UK and meets the people who look after them, beginning with Great Dixter in Northiam Village in the Sussex High Weald. Head gardener Fergus Garrett talks about how he endeavours to use the grounds as a living laboratory of horticultural experimentation, and encourage biodiversity of plant and animal species.(n)

programme.title: 5 News Weekend
programme.desc: Round-up of the day's headlines from around the world.(n)

programme.title: Cruising with Jane McDonald
programme.desc: Highlights of the singer's voyages around the Mediterranean, which has seen her visit places including Rome, Florence, Pisa, Monte Carlo, Monaco and Barcelona.(n)

programme.title: Princess Diana: In Her Own Words
programme.desc: Documentary charting Diana's royal journey, examining how she transformed herself from royal wife to global humanitarian. The programme features rarely-seen archive footage, as well as interviews with friends and acquaintances, including her former press secretary Patrick Jephson, royal correspondent and confidante Jennie Bond, and astrologer and close friend Deborah Frank.(n)

programme.title: Thomas Markle: Meghan & Me
programme.desc: Filmed at his home in Mexico in October 2019, this documentary follows Thomas Markle as he reveals the story behind his dramatic fall-out with daughter Meghan. Following the recent announcement that the Duke and Duchess of Sussex have chosen to step back as senior members of the royal family, Thomas gives his thoughts on their decision. Markle reveals his belief that there's a high likelihood he'll never see his daughter again, saying he misses 'the way that she was', and also claims he lied about a phone conversation with his daughter and son-in-law during a TV interview with Good Morning Britain's Piers Morgan.(n)

programme.title: The Tin Star (1957)
programme.desc: A world-weary bounty hunter, formerly a sheriff, arrives in a small town where a newly-appointed lawman is struggling to keep the peace. When he learns of the mercenary's past, the inexperienced lawman seeks his help in learning the tricks of the trade so he can bring an outlaw gang to justice. Anthony Mann's Western, starring Henry Fonda, Anthony Perkins, Lee Van Cleef, Betsy Palmer and Michel Ray.(n)

programme.title: Fair Wind to Java (1953)
programme.desc: A sailor races to reach a hoard of diamonds stowed on board a Dutch vessel that has sunk in the East Indies. However, the derelict ship lies close to an island inhabited by a fierce tribe with a deep mistrust of strangers, while a volcano is about to erupt at any moment. Seafaring adventure, with Fred MacMurray, Vera Ralston and Robert Douglas.(n)

programme.title: Rawhide (1951)
programme.desc: Outlaws hold staff and a passenger and her baby hostage at a remote stagecoach station while awaiting the arrival of a shipment of $100,000 in gold bullion. The nervous captives try to stay calm, but the tension between the gang leader and a jumpy henchman grows fraught, ending in a frantic shoot-out - with the infant in the thick of the fray. Western, starring Tyrone Power, Susan Hayward and Jack Elam.(n)

programme.title: The Bridges at Toko-Ri (1954)
programme.desc: An American naval reserve pilot is called up for service in the Korean War, and must leave his family and civilian job as a lawyer. In command of a corps of naval pilots, he heads a bombing campaign on strategic Communist positions, but later comes to question the morality of the conflict. Adventure, starring William Holden, Grace Kelly, Fredric March and Mickey Rooney.(n)

programme.title: The Imitation Game (2014)
programme.desc: Biopic of mathematician Alan Turing, whose work during the Second World War translated seemingly unbreakable Nazi codes as well as pioneering the development of computer technology. In the years following the war, his life would be destroyed as his homosexuality was discovered, leaving him facing criminal charges. Starring Benedict Cumberbatch, Keira Knightley and Charles Dance.(n)

programme.title: The Green Mile (1999)
programme.desc: A convicted child-killer of towering height and massive build is condemned to death row, but astonishes the guards with his good nature and miraculous healing powers. They believe the gentle giant is incapable of crime, and emotions run high as they realise there is no hope of overturning his death sentence. Supernatural drama, based on a novel by Stephen King, starring Tom Hanks, Michael Clarke Duncan, David Morse and Harry Dean Stanton.(n)

programme.title: Grandma (2015)
programme.desc: A woman recovering from a break-up receives an unexpected visit from her granddaughter, who desperately needs to raise $600 before the end of the day. They visit a series of old contacts in search of a loan, which leads to long-buried secrets coming to light. Comedy drama, starring Lily Tomlin and Julia Garner.(n)

programme.title: Rafiki (2018)
programme.desc: Kena and Ziki are the daughters of two men who are political rivals in an upcoming Nairobi election, and when they start dating they have to be careful about keeping the relationship a secret. Romantic drama, starring Samantha Mugatsia and Sheila Munyiva. In Swahili and English.(n)

programme.title: The Man from the Alamo (1953)
programme.desc: A soldier serving at the battle of the Alamo is sent to warn his comrades' families of the impending danger, but ends up falsely branded a deserter. He tries to clear his name with acts of heroism, including tracking down the traitor who robbed the casualties after the battle was over. Western, starring Glenn Ford and Julia Adams.(n)

programme.title: The Day Will Dawn (1942)
programme.desc: A foreign correspondent working in occupied Norway is assigned to an RAF detail helping freedom fighters to sabotage an important German U-boat installation. He enlists the help of a local sea captain's daughter, but the plan goes wrong and he ends up in enemy hands, facing death by firing squad. Second World War adventure, with Hugh Williams, Deborah Kerr and Ralph Richardson.(n)

programme.title: Only the Valiant (1951)
programme.desc: A tough cavalry officer selects six unlikely comrades to undertake a vital mission to defend an isolated fort against marauding Apaches. His men's initial hostility soon turns to respect, however, as they realise his wily ways will see them through the conflict. Western adventure based on a novel by Charles Marquis Warren, starring Gregory Peck, Barbara Payton, Ward Bond and Lon Chaney Jr.(n)

programme.title: Heaven Knows, Mr Allison (1957)
programme.desc: A US marine corporal stationed on a South Pacific island and a novice nun on a mission are forced into the hills when Japanese forces arrive and set up military bases during the Second World War. Before long the pair develop strong emotional ties, but in resisting their feelings for each other the relationship becomes one of deep mutual respect. Romantic drama, written and directed by John Huston, with Robert Mitchum and Deborah Kerr.(n)

programme.title: X-Men: The Last Stand (2006)
programme.desc: The mutant heroes return to face a battle on three fronts. Arch-enemy Magneto is back with an army of followers, while a corporation has created a drug that permanently robs mutants of their powers. Meanwhile, their former ally Jean Grey has returned from the dead as the destructive and seemingly unstoppable Phoenix. The third instalment of the superhero saga, starring Hugh Jackman, Ian McKellen, Famke Janssen, Halle Berry and Kelsey Grammer.(n)

programme.title: Dirty Grandpa (2016)
programme.desc: An uptight lawyer preparing to marry his boss's controlling daughter and secure a prestigious job is persuaded to drive his fun-loving grandfather to Florida. However, the old man is convinced his grandson is about to make a terrible mistake, and turns the road trip into a wildly hedonistic weekend in the hope of inspiring him to get more fun out of life. Comedy, starring Robert De Niro and Zac Efron.(n)

programme.title: Blue Is the Warmest Colour (2013)
programme.desc: A 15-year-old girl falls in love with an older woman and tentatively begins a relationship. They remain together for years to come, as the teenager grows to adulthood and is inspired by her partner to explore her desires and develop her own outlook on the world. Romantic drama, with Lea Seydoux and Adele Exarchopoulos. In French and English.(n)

programme.title: Ice Age: The Meltdown (2006)
programme.desc: Sid the sloth, Manny the mammoth and Diego the sabre-toothed tiger grimly realise the good life is about to come to an abrupt end as flood waters threaten their homes. However, a vulture suggests they could turn an old giant tree into a boat and make their escape, so the three friends set off in search of it. Animated adventure sequel, with the voices of Ray Romano, John Leguizamo and Denis Leary.(n)

programme.title: Rugrats in Paris: The Movie (2000)
programme.desc: The lucky little tots embark on a trip to a French theme park - but while the rest of the gang are intent on enjoying themselves, Chuckie is distracted by his wish for a new mum. He sets his sights on the owner of the amusement park as a prospective partner for his single dad. Animated adventure, with the voices of Christine Cavanaugh, Elizabeth Daily, Cheryl Chase, Kath Soucie and Susan Sarandon.(n)

programme.title: Ever After: A Cinderella Story (1998)
programme.desc: A girl grows up in 16th-century Europe under the iron fist of her stepmother, until the love of handsome Prince Henry offers a glimmer of hope. Unfortunately, the royal gent has worries of his own in the shape of an arranged marriage. Luckily, Renaissance genius Leonardo da Vinci is on hand to play fairy godfather and bring the pair together. Romantic drama, starring Drew Barrymore, Anjelica Huston, Dougray Scott and Timothy West.(n)

programme.title: Back to the Future (1985)
programme.desc: Teenager Marty McFly travels back to the 1950s in a time machine built by his eccentric inventor friend. The return journey proves tricky enough, but he also accidentally alters the sequence of events that led to his parents falling in love - and has to persuade his future dad to win his mother's heart, or he will never be born. Sci-fi comedy adventure, starring Michael J Fox, Christopher Lloyd, Lea Thompson and Crispin Glover.(n)

programme.title: Behind Enemy Lines (2001)
programme.desc: A reconnaissance pilot is shot down and becomes stranded in enemy territory after filming evidence of a genocidal massacre by Serb militia in Bosnia. The naval air commander for Nato forces in the Balkans defies orders by mounting an unauthorised mission to rescue him, while the airman faces a battle to stay alive as he is pursued by paramilitary forces. Bosnian War drama, starring Gene Hackman, Owen Wilson, Gabriel Macht and Olek Krupa.(n)

programme.title: A Good Day to Die Hard (2013)
programme.desc: John McClane goes to Russia having received word that his estranged son Jack is in trouble with the law. He is surprised to discover Jack is secretly a CIA agent, and father and son join forces to take on the Russian Mob, who are engaged in a plot to steal nuclear weapons. Action adventure sequel, starring Bruce Willis and Jai Courtney.(n)

programme.title: The Strangers: Prey at Night (2018)
programme.desc: Ten years after events of the first film, in a secluded Ohio trailer park, three masked strangers, Dollface, Pin-Up Girl, and the Man in the Mask, terrorise a family. Slasher horror sequel, starring Bailee Madison, Lewis Pullman and Christina Hendricks.(n)

programme.title: Rules of Engagement
programme.desc: Audrey takes Jen to a Bon Jovi concert, and all hell breaks loose when Jeff organises a barbecue on the roof and does not invite Adam. Timmy is asked by Russell to track down a girl for him, but when Timmy decides to date her himself, he has to face his friend's wrath.(n)

programme.title: Rules of Engagement
programme.desc: Jen accidentally puts the wrong date on Audrey's baby shower invitations, and is forced to create a makeshift celebration in a hurry. However, things do not go to plan when Audrey feels left out at the event due to her pregnant surrogate getting all of the attention. Comedy, starring Megyn Price.(n)

programme.title: Sabrina the Teenage Witch
programme.desc: Sabrina and Roxie are teamed together to write a story in an effort to land a sought-after spot on the school newspaper.(n)

programme.title: Sabrina the Teenage Witch
programme.desc: Salem falls for a college girl, while Sabrina throws a Hallowe'en party - where Roxie jeopardises the relationship between Frankenstein's monster and his gruesome bride. Supernatural sitcom, starring Melissa Joan Hart.(n)

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Mel asks Joe to act as a translator when she has a meeting with a Japanese businessman, but things turn a little awkward when they all get together for dinner. Meanwhile, Lennox and her nemesis are forced to work on a school project together. Starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence.(n)

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Despite everything that has happened to him over the past year, Joe decides to go to his college reunion to see his former classmates. However, when his date for the event cancels on him, Mel feels she must try to find a solution to the problem. Meanwhile, Lennox and Ryder plan to throw a party. Starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence.(n)

programme.title: Baby Daddy
programme.desc: Ben, Riley, Danny and Tucker wonder where their own lives are heading when Bonnie delegates a very important job to them. Riley feels conflicted about her relationships with Ben and Danny, and Bonnie makes a decision that leaves everyone feeling frustrated.(n)

programme.title: Baby Daddy
programme.desc: Bonnie promotes Riley to chief bridesmaid and demands a crazy hen party, while Brad asks Danny to be his best man and throw an epic stag do. Tucker tells everyone he's going to a television convention in Detroit, but he's actually making his annual trek to Las Vegas, where he spends one weekend a year living his dream of being a lounge singer. Dan Castellaneta guest stars.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: Baby Daddy

Riley finds some old home movies of the gang in high school, but discovers more than a trip down memory lane. programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine

programme.title: The Goldbergs

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Amy has to go undercover at a women's prison and befriend one of the inmates in order to try to get information about a criminal operation. Back at the precinct, Holt and the team plan a fake funeral in an attempt to catch a hitman.(n)

programme.desc: Sheldon and Amy stay up all night as they await a telephone call that could bring big news from the Nobel Committee - is everything in their lives about to change?(n)

programme.desc: The swashbuckling feline joins forces with a cunning thief and a treacherous old friend to steal a magical golden goose from a giant's castle. Animated comedy spin-off from the Shrek movies, with the voices of Antonio Banderas and Salma Hayek.(n)

programme.title: The Wolverine (2013)
programme.desc: The former member of the X-Men is lured out of hiding and travels to Japan, where a dying businessman offers him the chance to give up his immortality for a normal life. He ends up robbed of his superhuman powers by a mysterious scientist and is forced to protect the tycoon's granddaughter from a secret conspiracy's assassins. Superhero adventure spin-off, starring Hugh Jackman and Tao Okamoto.(n)

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The amateur critics share their opinions on TV from February 2017, including The Jump, Dragons' Den, Death in Paradise, Jon Richardson: How to Survive the End of the World, SS-GB and the news. Craig Cash narrates.(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance for viewers to purchase goods from the comfort of home.(n)

programme.title: Kirstie's Vintage Gems

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Childhood sweethearts Debbie and Tony Parkes have been married for 40 years, and are ready for a new chapter in their lives. With retirement around the corner, they've set their hearts on a holiday home in Albufeira on Portugal's Algarve with a budget of £80,000.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole makes good money restoring an antique pine sideboard in Sheffield, but has less success with a pair of old brass jugs, while Simon converts a staircase into shelves.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien visit Hampshire and Surrey, where Henry makes a pair of coffee tables from a cable drum and a galvanised water tank, and Simon rescues a winged back chair and upholsters it with his own designer fabric.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge celebrates his birthday with a hot-air balloon ride over the chateau at sunrise, before setting to work in the kitchen making his own jam and alcohol from the vast quantities of fresh fruit waiting to be harvested in the garden. Angel Adoree starts work on a new project of her own - building a geodesic dome on top of a floating platform.(n)

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton meets Kevin and Liz Davies, who plan to build a five-bedroom home complete with a swimming pool in north Somerset. They are expecting to spend around £610,000, but realise they must rethink the sink with a view and woodburner when the presenter casts his eye over the designs.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest begins at the King's Head in Addlethorpe, Lincolnshire, where landlady Helen Coy and her bar manager and best friend Emma Green are pulling out all the stops to win. The B&B owners are taken back to their childhood at the funfair, At dinner, B&B beginners Louise and Andy Macbeth rattle nerves when Louise reveals her past career as a hospitality journalist - leaving pub veteran Helen less than impressed.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: B&B newcomers Andy and Louise Macbeth invite their rival hoteliers to spend the night at the dog-friendly Beck Hall in North Yorkshire, where an extra-critical Helen and Emma discover what they believe to be stray dog hairs in their bed. A fishing trip makes Terri feel a little out of sorts, and when the time arrives for feedback, the managers are surprised to learn two of their guests have indicated they would not come back for a second stay.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to the Albaston in Torquay for the third day of the competition, where they are welcomed by owners and avid souvenir collectors, Kevin and Julie Shadbolt. Terri and Stuart are impressed by their room, but are startled by the memorabilia in the communal lounge, and expectations are raised at dinner when Kevin reveals he has a secret recipe for success when it comes to breakfast. However, at feedback the next day, tensions begin to rise.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final stay of the week is in Bournemouth, at Terri and Stewart's specialist dance holiday venue the Hotel Piccadilly, where a class in the grand ballroom soon has the guests in a spin. However, temperatures rise over dinner when the simmering tension between Louise and Helen finally boils over.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The owners meet up for the last time to find out what they've been paid and to settle some scores. King's Head landlady Helen challenges Louise and Andy about the problems they reported on cleanliness in the bathroom. while Louise and Andy from Beck Hall get hot under the collar when the others criticise their dog-friendly approach.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon O'Brien restores an enamel roll-top bath and builds a den out of old doors. Henry Cole makes a wall mounted safe from a jerry can and lovingly restores a cheese-making trough.(n)

programme.title: The Yorkshire Dales and the Lakes
programme.desc: Champion sheep breeder Arnold Lancaster prepares his flock for Westmorland County Show, while in the Dales, dog trainer John Halstad prepares his dog Wren for a trial.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: As Dick and Angel prepare for winter, they attend a food festival in Rouen, where they hope to find inspiration over what to do with their fresh fruit and vegetables. Angel adds the finishing touches to the floating geodesic dome in time for a family sleepover, while Dick gets to work fixing the crumbling stucco.(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows the progress of Ian and Sophie Cooper, who aim to restore a 200-year-old industrial building in Somerset. The couple plan to build a large glass extension to make the most of the incredible views on offer, but run into trouble when they learn the building has no foundations.(n)

programme.title: Outlander
programme.desc: As Roger arrives in Wilmington looking for Brianna, Jamie and Claire must keep Governor Tryon from ambushing Murtagh and the Regulators. Period drama, starring Caitriona Balfe, Sophie Skelton and Richard Rankin.(n)

programme.desc: Dougal and Ted visit the mainland, where they bump into actor Richard Wilson - and discover the hard way that he does not take kindly to being confused with his comic alter ego Victor Meldrew. Dermot Morgan and Ardal O'Hanlon star.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Ex-paratrooper Martin, who's 48, has sustained multiple life-changing injuries to his neck, pelvis and legs after his paraglider was blown into a hillside, and is rushed to hospital by emergency helicopter. Meanwhile, doctors treat 73-year-old Eddie for severe sepsis, and 72-year-old Graham, suffering from a constant nose bleed due to the blood-thinners he is taking, is also treated.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun

Laura Hamilton shows them five properties for their £110,000 budget around the Mojacar area on Spain's Costa de Almería.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien head to Redditch where Henry restores a 1970s Kawasaki motorbike, while Simon takes a pair of rusty cinema seat supports and creates a desirable garden bench.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau

Angel adds the finishing touches to the floating geodesic dome in time for a family sleepover, while Dick gets to work fixing the crumbling stucco.(n)

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton meets Mark and Abigail Smyth, who plan to save £130,000 by building a barn-style home in a quiet Warwickshire village. The presenter offers several ideas on how the couple can adapt their plans, and they also talk to Jeremy Spratley, who has carried out a similar project in Buckinghamshire.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest begins at Wharfe House in Wetherby, run by B&B newcomer Pauline Cox, who's joined by her best friend Claire Smith. Eyebrows are raised when Mike and Karen Hepker arrive from Bath Lodge Castle in a chauffeur-driven car. During a visit to a local vineyard, sparks fly between Mike Hepker and Tash Thorne, who runs The Old Lodge Hotel in Gosport with her partner Mike Pearce. Having only just opened, Pauline has a house full for the very first time. The narrow antique beds cause concerns and Jenny and Clive Aylett from Wortwell Hall Barn in Norfolk are first to criticise breakfast. When it comes to feedback, Pauline's left deflated by her sausage scores - and Karen and Mike Hepker vote with their feet.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners visit Wortwell Hall Barn in Norfolk, run by husband-and-wife duo Clive and Jenny Aylett, but when the pair's rivals arrive, Pauline Cox and Claire Smith have difficulty locating their favourite appliance. Publicans Mike Pearce and Tash Thorne are astonished to discover they cannot lock their room's door. In the morning, some of the guest make awkward requests to change their breakfast orders, causing professional chef Clive to experience a meltdown in the kitchen that leads to a fundamental error.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to Bath Lodge Castle in Somerset for the third day of the competition, where they are wowed by Karen and Mike Hepker's luxury castle experience. Although the guests are disappointed at the bathroom fixtures and fittings, they are given the chance to vent their emotions during an afternoon of painting in the castle grounds. Plus, tensions rise between Mike and Tash at dinner, and harsh comments at feedback lead the hosts to suspect game playing.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit of the week is to The Old Lodge Hotel in Gosport, Hampshire, run by publicans Mike Pearce and Tash Thorne. Karen Hepker is disappointed with the honeymoon suite, while Clive and Jenny Aylett find a dubious looking splash guard in the shower. A boat tour backfires when Jenny struggles with her water phobia and at dinner Karen takes the opportunity to tell the others exactly what she thinks of them. But things really kick off when it comes to feedback time.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Wharfe House hosts Pauline Cox and Claire Smith begin a bitter debate, demanding to know why their beds caused such a sleepless night. Accusations subsequently fly in all directions and walk-outs look on the cards as tensions mount leading to the payments being revealed.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien visit Herefordshire and Worcestershire. Henry makes a table from the base of a broken agricultural grain mill, while Simon creates a garden seat from an old mangle.(n)

programme.title: The Yorkshire Dales and the Lakes
programme.desc: In the Dales, John Dawson of Bleak Bank farm leaves his son William in charge on a trip away. William gets baling during a break in the weather. In the Lake District, GP Stuart Allen attempts a 50-mile ultra-run that stretches the entire length of the Lakes. Back in the Dales, geologist John Helm is on a mission to ensure members of the public can safely descend into Gaping Gill, one of the biggest and most spectacular underground caves in Britain.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Dick and Angel expand the project by setting up a luxury campsite on the chateau grounds, but they only have six weeks to complete it before the first guests arrive - and the weather is not on their side, with constant rain turning the planned site into a muddy field. They also have to contend with a visit from Dick's family at Easter.(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Londoner Chris Ostwald decides to build a New England-style water mill in the Chilterns, on a hill and nowhere near any water, after being inspired by an illustration on a yoghurt pot. He bought the plot 20 years previously and it has always been his dream to build a retreat for himself and his family. Everything seems to be going smoothly, until a neighbour complains the house is breaking its planning consent. Kevin McCloud presents.(n)

programme.title: Code 37: Sex Crimes
programme.desc: An air of uncertainty hangs around the team after the death of Superintendent Vermaelen. However, officers don't have much time to think about consequences as a young girl, Trui, is found dead at a boarding school. One of her friends tells Hannah that Trui had been raped in her room. The squad starts by investigating the role of Sister Adelheid, one of the nuns who teaches at the school. Veerle Baetens stars. In Flemish.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Glyn, 53, is brought in by ambulance after a work-related accident on a building site, and in his hour of need he turns to his lifelong friend Barrie. Eighteen-month-old Zemirah's parents are concerned with her diarrhoea and vomiting and 93-year-old John is admitted after falling at home, but also has a severe case of sepsis.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Danny, who's 41, is brought into hospital after falling 20 feet through a skylight onto concrete. Meanwhile, 27-year-old Tara receives treatment for her severe abdominal pains, and three-year-old Clara is brought to A&E by her mum, with a chickpea stuck up her nose.(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: In the concluding part of his trip to the Mill Street Bistro in Norwalk, Ohio, Gordon's disagreement with proprietor Joe Nagy erupts into one of his most intense confrontations yet. Just when it looks like he has turned the corner and is ready to accept help, Joe slips back into his old ways, denying his own failings and picking petty fights. Can Gordon get this stubborn owner to finally accept his advice and secure the future of his restaurant?(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Glyn, 53, is brought in by ambulance after a work-related accident on a building site, and in his hour of need he turns to his lifelong friend Barrie. programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty heads on the trail of an elusive fruit - the fresh date, visiting a vast water purification plant in Israel where the raw sewage of Tel Aviv is harnessed to turn barren desert into fertile farmland. Kate Quilton flies to California to investigate why almond butter is more expensive than other nutty spreads, where she discovers a combination of extreme weather and global economics have affected the price of almonds. Matt Tebbutt makes a surprising discovery about the true origins of haggis.(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance for viewers to purchase goods from the comfort of home.(n)

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: The team members select some of their favourite investigations from the show. Putting plums, prunes and prune juice to the test, Jimmy Doherty heads to King's College London to make a concoction that will help keep people's bowel movements regular. Kate Quilton visits Vietnam to learn about the difference between black and white pepper, and discovers the remarkable lengths to which producers go to guard their valuable crop from thieves, while Matt Tebbutt is in Switzerland to find out why there are holes in Swiss cheese.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Angie and Sandy from Nottingham are approaching retirement and looking for a place where they can spend quality time together. They've decided the sunshine of the southern Costa Blanca is the perfect place to do just that, so Ben Hillman shows them five properties for their £75,000 budget.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Recently retired Alan and Jackie Patrick from Hampshire are keen to buy a property on the north of Spain's Costa Blanca in the Marina Alta region, close to where Jackie's brothers live. Ben Hillman show them a range of properties for their £85,000 budget, but will any character town houses have them reaching for their chequebook?(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Farzana Kausar from Stockport, Greater Manchester, is looking for a better quality of life in the Spanish sun. Her daughter Aisha, who uses a wheelchair, is also making the full-time move. With a budget of £120,000 for the property and any alterations they'll need to make for Aisha, Laura Hamilton shows them five homes around Torrevieja on the Costa Blanca.(n)

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp helps a couple looking for a home in Warrington, who do not seem to be able to agree on the best approach to house hunting, which has led to six months with no progress. In Liverpool, Phil Spencer comes to the aid of a successful entrepreneur looking for a house in Crosby, but who has very specific ideas.(n)

programme.title: Bake Off: The Professionals
programme.desc: Liam Charles and Tom Allen set two more challenges to the five remaining teams. Firstly, they must produce 24 perfect and identical layered vegetable cake slices and tomato shaped desserts in just three and a half hours. In the second stage, they must make baskets out of 160 choux buns, which are then filled with everything you might find at an allotment - but all made from sugar. Benoit Blin and Cherish Finden are on hand to assess the results and decide which teams go through to the next round.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Taxi driver Nik Christides hosts the first party in east Hampshire, where one guest causes a stir by revealing details of a romantic encounter with Boyzone singer Ronan Keating. Later, Nik introduces his diners to the sport of Cheesy Darts, which requires one guest to smear their face with cream, before having cheese-flavoured crisps thrown at them.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Mother-of-four Jax Naudi hosts the second dinner party in east Hampshire, but her theme for the evening causes confusion and leads to one guest wearing an inappropriate outfit. Although Sue Hood expresses her distaste for the host's menu by remaining silent throughout the meal, the mood improves when Jax reveals the evening's entertainment - life drawing with a nude model.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Natalie Edemska-Plant hosts the third dinner party in east Hampshire, and plans to serve dishes from her childhood to give her guests an authentic Russian dining experience. She begins the evening with traditional customs such as banning shoes inside the house, and although her food is not a complete success, the night ends on a high note with some energetic dancing.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: History fanatic Kyle Curtis hosts the fourth dinner party from east Hampshire. A self-confessed novice in the kitchen, he decides to get his guests to help him with his main course - a fish fondue - and his choice of dress code for the evening proves a hit.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Sue Hood hosts the final night of the culinary competition in east Hampshire, hoping to impress her guests with a Caribbean-themed dinner party. The evening kicks off with the diners taking turns hitting a pinata, followed by entertainment in the form of dancers before the winner of the £1,000 prize is announced.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest begins at Wizards Thatch in Alderley Edge, a village in Cheshire. Owners Ian Lambert and his partner Ali Imiolek hope to cast a spell over the competition, but their guests end up picking out problems with the continental breakfast basket.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition moves to Blackpool, where James Reay and Simon Dooley run the one-star Winterbourne Hotel and Theatre Showbar. The contestants seem to be relaxed and impressed, but a rival's feedback sends one of the hosts storming out of the room.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Perfectionist Andrea Hickmott and her partner Nick Bonner-Williams welcome their guests to Northrise Lodge in Hastings, East Susses, but the rooms are criticised and deemed too clinical to relax in. Andrea also panics when she forgets to put sugar on the tables at breakfast.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Stephanie and Dominic Parker (who would go on to find fame in Gogglebox) welcome the rest of the contestants to their B&B, the Salutation in Sandwich, Kent. However, an alcohol-fuelled evening of parlour games jeopardises the hosts' chances to win the prize.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to discover how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers and to air their grievances. A wedding proposal appears to be on the cards, and the winner is announced.(n)

programme.title: Devon and Cornwall
programme.desc: High up on Dartmoor, shepherds Steve and Crispin Alford gather their sheep for a speed-shearing competition. It's a big day for Steve, as it's the first time he's hosted the annual charity event on his farm. In east Devon, master wheelwrights Greg and Mike Rowland embark on a new commission - building a gun carriage to sit aboard the HMS Warrior, while on the south coast of Cornwall, foraging chef Greg Milne wanders the woodland in search of inspiration for a new dish to serve at his restaurant on St Michael's Mount.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Cameras follow 54-year-old Richard, who arrives at St George's hospital suffering symptoms of a suspected stroke. Meanwhile, 76-year-old Peter turns up in A&E with a severe head injury, covered in blood, but it's not clear what happened to him.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: This episode focuses on the chances people take in life and whether a gamble ever pays off. 20-year-old Megan is rushed to St George's hospital after being involved in a high speed car crash, and as doctors begin treatment and work to assess her injuries, it soon becomes clear how her hedonistic lifestyle has put her at risk. Meanwhile, James, who's 78, is transferred from his local hospital with suspected internal bleeding, and 40-year-old Jibraeel has lodged a cotton bud in his ear.(n)

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: Key striker Father Jack drinks himself into oblivion just as the Craggy Island clerics are about to play a vital five-a-side football match against their Rugged Island rivals, led by devious Dick Byrne. Dermot Morgan stars.(n)

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: Cheating in the football match backfires on Ted, prompting his Rugged Island rivals to think up a devilish forfeit - and leaving the priest trying to contrive a way of kicking Bishop Brennan's backside and getting away with it. Comedy, starring Dermot Morgan and Ardal O'Hanlon.(n)

programme.title: Storage Hunters
programme.desc: Brandon wins some spy equipment at an auction in Culver City.(n)

programme.title: Storage Hunters
programme.desc: Jesse forges controversial alliances, while Sean uncovers an auction lot that has been used for a prank.(n)

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: The guys go big in Texas with a mega pick at the first Dr Pepper bottling plant, and tough negotiations in a 50,000 square-foot warehouse.(n)

programme.desc: Highlights of the offbeat event, where teams create vehicles without engines and race downhill.(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Richard Hammond and James May test the Porsche Panamera against the Royal Mail's delivery service, racing a first-class letter to see which travels faster over the full length of the British Isles. Jeremy Clarkson gets chased by the British Army in some of their most deadly toys, and multi Olympic gold medal-winning sprinter Usain Bolt is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car.(n)

programme.title: World's Most Dangerous Roads
programme.desc: Ben Fogle and Hugh Dennis undertake a perilous journey through the Peruvian Andes. Their route takes in 2,000ft drops, one-lane dirt roads, remote mountain trails and muddy, humid jungle terrain, as they search for the lost city of Constitucion, once destined to be the capital of Peru.(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's French Odyssey
programme.desc: Rick's barge trip brings him to Castelnaudary, where he visits the canteen of the French Foreign Legion, prepares a spicy tagine with couscous and enjoys a three-course lunch consisting of roast leg of veal with potato gratin dauphinioise, goats' cheese and strawberries.(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's French Odyssey
programme.desc: The connoisseur of local food starts to smell the salty Mediterranean sea on the warm breeze and serves freshly grilled sardines with salad and cold rose wine for the boat crew as they reach Narbonne. Meanwhile, Louis, the chef onboard Anjodi, rustles up seafood with squid ink pasta.(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May each buy a rear-wheel-drive coupe for £1,500 and find themselves entered into a French ice race. Richard discovers a way to fill the waiting time for flights by inventing the sport of airport-vehicle racing, Jeremy gets behind the wheel of the BMW Z4, and actress Sienna Miller is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car.(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May are each challenged to find a classic car at auction for £3,000. Exploring whether they are more fun to drive than modern alternatives, the trio then take the vehicles to Majorca to compete in a rally. Jeremy also test-drives the BMW Z4 and Nissan 370Z, and AC/DC frontman Brian Johnson is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car.(n)

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Beach balls, water and sand contribute to a seaside feel to this episode, a jolly tone that is interrupted when Greg Davies deals with the questionable actions of one contestant.(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Samantha Womack, Rory Reid, Anne Diamond and James Acaster continue the daily test of their general knowledge skills, with Richard Osman.(n)

programme.title: Room 101
programme.desc: Frank Skinner invites actor Ross Kemp, singer Sophie Ellis-Bextor and comedian Rob Beckett to vent their spleen about people and objects that upset them, as the trio vie to banish their top peeves, annoyances, irritation or worst nightmares to the depths of Room 101 for ever. Their gripes include misplaced clapping, teeth, expensive water in hotel rooms and snobby shop assistants.(n)

programme.title: Room 101
programme.desc: Frank Skinner invites Kaiser Chiefs' frontman Ricky Wilson, and comedians Joe Lycett and Zoe Lyons to express their anger over people and objects that upset them, as the trio vie to banish their top peeves, annoyances, irritation or worst nightmares consigned to oblivion. Their gripes include new towels, escalators and the bartender at the local pub.(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Comedian Rhod Gilbert hosts the stand-up show at London's Hammersmith Apollo, welcoming Liverpudlian John Bishop as his guest, with both comics sharing their light-hearted views on modern life.(n)

programme.title: Mock the Week

The panellists give their take on the week's major news stories and participate in a series of stand-up spots and improvised games. First shown in 2014.(n)

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Paul Sinha, Lou Sanders and Iain Stirling become one while Greg Davies watches on, ready to issue his judgements, while Sian Gibson seeks to redefine the definition of 'bounce'.(n)

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Extended edition. programme.title: Room 101

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May are each challenged to find a classic car at auction for £3,000. Exploring whether they are more fun to drive than modern alternatives, the trio then take the vehicles to Majorca to compete in a rally. Jeremy also test-drives the BMW Z4 and Nissan 370Z, and AC/DC frontman Brian Johnson is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car.(n)

programme.title: World's Most Dangerous Roads
programme.desc: Celebrities undertake some of the most perilous journeys in the world. Comedians Ed Byrne and Andy Parsons drive along Siberia's M56 Kolyma highway, known as the 'Road of Bones'. Along the way, they brave sub-zero temperatures, traverse icy routes, encounter deserted gulags and meet the locals who inhabit the frozen wilderness, but despite picking up practical tips for survival, the duo feel they are out of their depth.(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's French Odyssey
programme.desc: The chef journeys along the Etang de Thau, famous for its oysters and mussels, before mooring his barge in the port of Sete, where Bastille Day is being celebrated. He also rustles up seafood ragout and roast black bream before travelling on to Marseille to tackle the most famous fish dish of all - bouillabaisse.(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's French Odyssey
programme.desc: The chef's epic journey from the Atlantic to the Mediterranean comes to an end as he takes the final leg of his gastronomic tour along the canal systems of southern France. Arriving in Marseille, he samples seafood and regional cuisine, as well as showing a variety of traditional recipes. He also cooks roast black bream and finds time to send Chalky a postcard.(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson and James May are challenged to create a TV advert for Volkswagen, but soon fall out as James concentrates on detailed information on fuel economy and Jeremy wants something more exciting. Richard Hammond explores an alternative to Germanic sports saloons and tests the Vauxhall VXR8 Bathurst, and former US chat show host Jay Leno is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car.(n)

programme.title: Top Gear

programme.title: Rick Stein: From Venice to Istanbul
programme.desc: The chef's gastronomic road trip brings him to northern Greece where he enjoys traditional dishes including veal stiffado and kotopita - which Rick declares to be the best chicken pie he has ever tasted. In the coastal city of Preveza he goes out prawn fishing and follows in the footsteps of poet Lord Byron visiting Messolonghi, a city where seafood is a highly regarded commodity. Rick also rolls up his own sleeves to cook his favourite Greek dishes of stuffed peppers and tomatoes and souvlaki.(n)

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: The guys visit a jaw-dropping Kentucky property with a racetrack that revives an old Mike and Frank rivalry. Later, a freestyle stop in North Carolina looks like a bust.(n)

programme.title: American Pickers

programme.title: Top Gear

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Motor racing driver Lewis Hamilton demonstrates his abilities on the test track, and Richard Hammond tries his hand in a Formula One car. Jeremy Clarkson and James May assess historic vehicles to establish which one pioneered the control layout used in modern cars, and singer-songwriter James Blunt is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car.(n)

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lucy offends father-in-law Frank by casting doubt on his ability to mind the children while she and Lee enjoy an evening out at the ballet. Sally Bretton co-stars, alongside veteran comedy performer Bobby Ball and Outnumbered's Hugh Dennis.(n)

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee, Lucy and the children set off in their car to catch a ferry with plenty of time to spare - or so they think. As a consequence their plans for a welcome break prove anything but stress-free. Rob Brydon guest stars, with Lee Mack and Sally Bretton.(n)

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee and Lucy play hot-tub fantasy date with Anna and Toby - and soon find themselves landing in hot water. One-time Spice Girl Emma Bunton and Countdown's resident lexicographer Susie Dent appear as themselves, alongside regulars Lee Mack, Sally Bretton, Hugh Dennis and Abigail Cruttenden.(n)

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Host Dara O Briain and regulars Hugh Dennis and Andy Parsons are joined by Rob Beckett, Ed Byrne, Milton Jones and Zoe Lyons on the topical comedy quiz. The panellists give their take on the week's major news stories and participate in a series of stand-up spots and improvised games. From 2014.(n)

programme.title: Big Zuu's Big Eats
programme.desc: Big Zuu and the boys are going international - flying all the way to the Isle of Man to cook for comedian Josh Widdicombe on his ‘Bit Much' tour.(n)

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain presents a special compilation show featuring previously unbroadcast material, out-takes and favourite bits from the recent series of the satirical comedy quiz, when regular panellists Andy Parsons and Hugh Dennis were joined by guests including Josh Widdicombe, Miles Jupp, Holly Walsh and Rob Beckett. From 2014.(n)

programme.title: The Day They Dropped the Bomb
programme.desc: Documentary marking the 70th anniversary of the day the world's first atomic bomb was detonated above the Japanese city of Hiroshima on August 6, 1945. Featuring a minute-by-minute account of what happened on that tragic day, through archive footage and interviews with eyewitnesses who rarely speak about their experience of the bombing. Also interviewed is navigator Theodore Van Kirk, who was the last surviving member of the crew who dropped the bomb, before his death in 2014.(n)

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A devastated cliff-top ruin, an extraordinarily tall tower, a concrete structure stranded at sea and a heavy metal monster in a field all feature.(n)

programme.title: Murder Maps
programme.desc: Part two of two. A re-examination of Jack the Ripper's crimes, revealing how the story known today was shaped by the sensationalist press of 1888. Featuring contributions from Ripper expert Donald Rumbelow and writer Hallie Rubenhold, author of The Five - the only book to tell the lives of the victims.(n)

programme.title: Nazi Murder Mysteries
programme.desc: The German police's attempts to catch a depraved and psychotic killer, who struck fear into women travelling on Berlin's S-Bahn train network during the Second World War.(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Tewkesbury Abbey, Gloucestershire. A visitor from Australia hopes a set of silver knives will be worth the cost of her plane ticket, while two sisters who split their great great grandmother's jewellery between them hope to find out who has the most valuable inheritance. Lennox Cato values a box that may have belonged to William Shakespeare's wife, and a collection of rare figures and dolls turns out to be a find of national importance.(n)

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A devastated cliff-top ruin, an extraordinarily tall tower, a concrete structure stranded at sea and a heavy metal monster in a field all feature.(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on the final leg of his journey through the Republic of Ireland, travelling from Athlone to Galway. Along the way he examines historic jewellery with royal connections, encounters a people's king and meets up with a traditional Irish singing group.(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Armed with a copy of George Bradshaw's Victorian Railway Guidebook, Michael Portillo embarks on another journey around the country to discover how the railways have affected people and communities, and the legacy they have left behind. He begins in Manchester, where he finds out how the world's first industrialised city produced a revolutionary political movement, and learns about the railway workers who founded one of the most successful football clubs of all time. Along the way, the presenter does the washing in Port Sunlight - a model village on the Wirral - and hears stories about the aptly named George Francis Train's time in Birkenhead, Merseyside.(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Audley End, Essex, where experts value a sword that played an important role in the end of the American War of Independence, a collection of toilet chains and a silver container that once contained a goat's gallstone believed to have magical powers. The biggest shock of the day comes when Eric Knowles reveals the worth of a set of three vases decorated with fairies.(n)

programme.title: Wild Africa
programme.desc: Documentary focusing the mountains of Africa, home to many rarely seen species. From the snow-covered peaks of Kilimanjaro to the Ethiopian Highlands where temperatures fall as low as -20C, wildlife continues to thrive, including the rat-catching wolves which revel in their rodent-infested territory and bone-smashing vultures with a taste for marrow. Unique habits and survival instincts are all on display as the unpredictability and numerous threats to mountain life are revealed.(n)

programme.title: Chris Tarrant: Extreme Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Chris sets out to reach the northernmost railway station on Earth, inside Russia's Arctic Circle. This is an epic 2,000-mile adventure from Moscow into Arctic Siberia, and begins with a marathon 19-hour leg. Along the way he enjoys a reindeer stew with a local family and takes the controls of one of the massive locomotives that power the trains, before pressing on along railways built under the brutal regime of Joseph Stalin.(n)

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A devastated cliff-top ruin, an extraordinarily tall tower, a concrete structure stranded at sea and a heavy metal monster in a field all feature.(n)

programme.title: Hitler's Propaganda Machine
programme.desc: The use of propaganda in Hitler's Nazi regime. The first episode examines the Fuehrer's fascination with propaganda, which began in the trenches of the First World War.(n)

programme.title: Nazi Murder Mysteries
programme.desc: The dark tale of the skeleton of an unknown woman found in a hollowed-out tree in Birmingham woodland, exploring the links between the woman and Nazi spy rings operating in Britain.(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his journey around north-west England in the elegant resort town of Southport, where the railways provided thousands of holidaymakers with the chance enjoy the fun of the fair. On his journey around some of the region's larger towns, the presenter uncovers the history of Victorian entrepreneurship in Wigan and the beginnings of the Industrial Revolution in Bolton, before heading to smaller Leyland to get behind the wheel of a 100-year-old commercial vehicle.(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his journey around north-west England by celebrating Victorian trade with the Preston Guild, and then heading to Rochdale to learn about a pioneering movement to improve the lives of working families. The presenter also follows in the tracks of many 19th-century industrial employees who made day trips to Hebden Bridge to walk in the Calder Valley.(n)

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: Claustrophobic prison cells that contain a story worthy of international acclaim, and a vast mountainside complex that held the power to change the world.(n)

programme.title: Secret Nazi Bases
programme.desc: As the Red Army approached a forest in Poland toward the end of the war, they stumbled upon a massive complex containing more than a thousand structures across 25sqkm.(n)

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Factors influencing America's decision to support Britain during the Second World War, and how Hitler's declaration of war on the US convinced Congress to take more decisive action. Narrated by Laurence Olivier.(n)

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: Harris accidentally drops stolen pills into Fletcher's midday cuppa - much to the annoyance of prison racketeer Harry Grout, who resents a newcomer muscling in on his patch. Classic comedy, starring Ronnie Barker.(n)

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: Fletcher objects to having a third cellmate foisted on him, especially as the newcomer seems to enjoy special privileges and the full backing of the wardens. Comedy, starring Ronnie Barker and Fulton Mackay.(n)

programme.title: Porridge

programme.title: Murder Maps
programme.desc: The gun battle on the thoroughfares of London in 1911 known as the Siege of Sidney Street. Four hundred rounds of ammunition were fired over six hours in one of the earliest news incidents to be captured by motion camera.(n)

The presenter also follows in the tracks of many 19th-century industrial employees who made day trips to Hebden Bridge to walk in the Calder Valley.(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents a second visit to Audley End House, Essex, where items up for valuation include a large bronze cockerel that was buried during both world wars to escape being melted down for ammunition and a table that Napoleon may have signed his abdication on. One visitor to the show has a fascination with design diagrams inspired by an earlier episode - and brings in a collection of 650.(n)

programme.title: Wild Africa
programme.desc: The savannah's mixture of grassy plains and woodlands boast an array of wildlife, ranging from many species of big cat to herds of buffalo, wildebeest and gazelle. The programme explores the way animals and people have shaped these lands.(n)

programme.title: Chris Tarrant: Extreme Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Chris attempts to cross the length of Cuba, the only island in the Caribbean to boast an extensive railway network, starting in Havana in the west. This proves to be a major challenge given the unreliability of the service and the old trains regularly used, but makes for a fascinating journey to Chris's ultimate goal, the American naval base at Guantanamo Bay. Along the way he soaks up the sights and sounds of Havana, retraces the footsteps of the country's national hero Che Guevara, and visits the ghost town of Camilo Cienfuegos - a relic from Cuba's colonial sugar plantation era.(n)

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: Hidden tunnels that saw a city prosper, a sunken wreck with a remarkable past, a concrete wartime dome and a mysterious Cold War building.(n)

programme.title: Hitler's Propaganda Machine
programme.desc: The decade of propaganda, impassioned speeches, newspaper articles, posters and campaigning that all led to one moment - Hitler being made Chancellor of Germany in January 1933.(n)

programme.title: Nazi Murder Mysteries
programme.desc: The theories surrounding the death of Hermann Goering, who managed to cheat the hangman by taking a cyanide capsule on the day before his scheduled death.(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo crosses into West Yorkshire to begin the next leg of his journey, visiting the Worth Valley and the home of the three Bronte sisters, who were early investors in the railway industry. He then heads to Oakworth to find out how its station and tracks were used in one of the most popular films ever made, and in Bradford discovers the ways in which 19th-century workers saved to buy a home. The presenter finishes in Halifax, where he learns how the railways contributed to the town's success.(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo tunes into the music of the mills and collieries of Victorian England by joining a brass band in Honley, West Yorkshire, before stopping off in Holmfirth to learn about a tragedy that led to a tourist boom in the town. The presenter then crosses into Derbyshire to pay homage to railway engineer George Stephenson at his resting place in Chesterfield, before concluding his journey at Chatsworth House, which was one of the first stately homes to welcome visitors by rail.(n)

programme.title: Secrets of the Solar System
programme.desc: In 1610, Galileo Galilei became the first person to observe Saturn's rings and this celestial mystery has been puzzling mankind ever since.(n)

programme.desc: Derek collects a Jaguar Mark 2 and explains why they made such poor getaway cars for 1960s bank robbers. A customer considers a Morris Marina as a Christmas present for his wife.(n)

programme.desc: Three rare Alvises get the auction buyers going when put under the hammer. A Chevy Corvette which started its journey in Saudi Arabia makes its way to Thornton le Dale for the sale.(n)

programme.title: Meat Loaf: In and Out of Hell
programme.desc: A profile of the rock star's life and career, reflecting on the music that propelled him to fame and charting his journey from an overweight, bullied child to his larger-than-life persona and icon status. Featuring an interview and performance by the singer, as well as backstage footage from a Las Vegas concert. The film also revisits the Dallas of Meat Loaf's early years and includes insights from high school friends who reveal where the unusual moniker really came from.(n)

programme.title: You've Got a Friend: The Carole King Story
programme.desc: The award-winning US singer gives an account of her life, looking back on her upbringing in Brooklyn, and the subsequent success she enjoyed as part of husband-and-wife songwriting team Goffin and King in the 1960s, including the creation of global hits Take Good Care of My Baby, The Locomotion and Will You Love Me Tomorrow. By 1970, Carole was divorced and had moved to Los Angeles, where she created her classic solo album Tapestry. Now in her seventies, she reveals herself to be as much an environmental activist as a musician, lobbying Congress in defence of the wildlife and eco-systems in her beloved Idaho.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away

programme.title: Frasier

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Lost Pyramids of the Aztecs
programme.desc: Part one of two. Archaeologists unearth the remains of the ancient civilisation from beneath Mexico City, digging deeper than ever before to find out who the Aztecs were and how they built their remarkable empire. The first episode features an attempt to build a replica of a pyramid, as well as investigating the mysterious pyramid ruins being unearthed in the heart of Mexico City and a journey into a set of underground tunnels that have lain undisturbed for centuries.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: This episode follows the stories of people who have lived through tragic, life-changing events, and explores how these individuals endure with the strength and support of their loved ones. On the paediatric ward, a mother has brought her 14-year-old daughter to see a doctor after she hurt her ankle during gymnastics.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A Gulf War veteran is arrested on suspicion of killing a peace campaigner whose views he opposed because he believed they were anti-American - and cites the recent death of his son in Afghanistan as the cause of his extreme emotional disturbance. Drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Jesse L Martin.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A investigation into multiple homicides at a clothes shop leads to the arrest of a schizophrenic man, and his decision to represent himself in court provides McCoy with a formidable opponent.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A baby is found dead in his cot, and reports indicate he was poisoned. All the evidence pins the blame on a British au pair employed by the parents, but she claims the infant's death was a result of the mother's negligence. Sam Waterston stars.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Briscoe and Green attend the scene of a double homicide at an exclusive restaurant. It transpires that one of the victims was a respected Hollywood producer - and had made enemies in the criminal underworld after filming a movie about the Mafia. Starring Jerry Orbach and Jesse L Martin.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A man with a history of violence is arrested for the murder of his son's school ice-hockey coach, but pleads mitigating circumstances. Helped by a glib defence lawyer, he claims that he committed the crime while suffering from a previously unknown mental condition he calls 'sports rage'.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Briscoe and Green investigate the discovery of four bodies in a jeweller's shop. Suspicion soon falls on the husband of one of the victims, who is revealed to have changed his identity to escape a shady past. The shop's security tapes are subsequently discovered in his flat, but the defendant surprises everyone by insisting on representing himself in court - and makes a big impression on the jury.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The American crime drama returns with the team still reeling from the Department of Defence investigation, with Ziva, McGee and Tony having handed in their badges. Parsons continues his inquiries into Gibbs and his agents, but events take a turn when an explosion kills the Secretary of the US Navy and seriously injures the director of Homeland Security. When attempts are made on the lives of Gibbs and Tony, it appears that all of the NCIS agents are in mortal danger. Starring Mark Harmon and Colin Hanks.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Sarah Porter, the new Secretary of the Navy, charges Gibbs and the team with finding terrorist group the Brotherhood of Doubt, which was responsible for killing her predecessor. She expects the group's next target to be billionaire Tomas Mendez, who spends his money trying to defeat terrorism, but whose motives may not be entirely altruistic. Meanwhile, Tony flies to Tel Aviv, where new Mossad director Orli Elbaz reveals they have a report of a possible sighting of Ziva at a kibbutz she went to as a child. Crime drama, guest starring Marina Sirtis.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs and the team investigate when naval cryptanalyst Andrew Wells is shot dead in a park outside Baltimore. At the scene, Tony spots a man who was one of his informants from 15 years earlier when he was a police officer, but who went into hiding after a bid to bring down the Russian mobsters he worked for went wrong. Drama, starring Mark Harmon.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team works with Abigail Borin of the Coast Guard Investigative Service after a highly suspicious explosion and fire on board an oil rig kills a US marine corps reservist. Abby detects malware in the platform's computer systems, while Ducky finds traces on the victim's body that lead to a lawyer who works for the oil company. Back in the office, a prankster is bringing some light relief and although the likeliest suspect is Tony, he denies any involvement. Crime drama, guest starring Diane Neal.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A pilot hijacks a commercial plane to escape from the man who attacked her, and Barba targets the airline for driving her to drastic action with an abusive working culture. Meanwhile, Fin takes steps to ensure Benson is protected at work.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A rape victim is found in the street - groggy after being drugged - and in her confused state she mistakenly accuses the wrong man of attacking her, with alarming consequences. The team sets up a trap in a bid to find the identity of the true criminal. Crime drama, starring Mariska Hargitay.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: When the assistant of a female videogame developer is attacked at a convention the team goes undercover online and discovers a world of misogynistic abuse by gamers. The detectives think they have found the perpetrators of the attack - but another attack is planned against the victim's employer.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: The drama about an NYPD team specialising in sexual crimes returns. Benson tries to juggle work and her new role as a foster mother to Noah Porter. Meanwhile, a new detective assigned to the unit gets off to a rocky start. Mariska Hargitay and Dann Florek star.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: The SVU intervenes in a million-dollar clothing deal when a basketball superstar is accused of rape - but the team members disagree on how to handle the case. Hard-hitting and emotional series chronicling the life and crimes of the New York Police Department, starring Mariska Hargitay and Dann Florek.(n)

programme.title: Bull
programme.desc: An attorney for one of Bull's former clients, Liberty Davis, asks him to consult on a couple's civil suit against a teacher who had a sexual relationship with their son.(n)

programme.title: Bull
programme.desc: Benny defends a whistle-blowing US Army analyst from a court-martial, but when he refuses to share redacted government secrets with his colleagues, trust issues begin to develop.(n)

programme.title: Criminals: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Journalist Nick Wallis joins police forces around the nation to discover how CCTV and technological advances are helping to bring criminals to justice. The programme reveals how essential surveillance cameras have become in the fight against crime and features footage of people caught red-handed.(n)

programme.title: Castle

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: McCoy vows to find those responsible when a key witness to the killing of a drug dealer is shot dead. Sam Waterson, Elisabeth Rohm and Jerry Orbach star.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Briscoe, Curtis, McCoy and Kincaid witness the execution of a criminal they helped bring to justice, but the event culminates in personal tragedies for each of them.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: The team investigates a disabled boy's death in an apartment fire, focusing inquiries not only on a tenant who lived in the same building and had been linked to several previous blazes, but also on the victim's parents, who had struggled to cope with the strain of looking after him. With Jerry Orbach, Dianne Wiest and guest star Rudolph Giuliani.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A bar-room brawl results in a baseball player being killed, leading Briscoe and Green to suspect a sporting grudge is behind the killing. However, a very different vendetta comes to light as the evidence points to a man recently exonerated after serving 20 years in prison for murder. Crime drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Jesse L Martin.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Animal rights activists are implicated in the death of an Aids researcher killed by a bite from an infected monkey, and use the ensuing court case to drum up support for their cause. As events unfold, McCoy and Carmichael find themselves debating ethics with lawyers who argue the protection of animal life is a valid defence in a murder case.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A woman's body is found in a Central Park lake during a Puerto Rican Day parade. The victim turns out to be the wife of a powerful billionaire, and pressure is put on the police to find the culprit quickly. Suspicion falls on the business partner of the victim's husband and a witness comes forward to identify him, making everything seem clear cut - but not for long.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: An explosion at an apartment building brings navy officer Terence Keith to the team's attention and it transpires he had been on medication for anxiety, a condition that had led to him being ejected from a pilot's training course. Ducky's profile of Keith suggests that he may be unstable and about to exact revenge for having his dream of becoming a fighter ace taken away from him, and evidence points to him intending to carry out a suicide attack.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs and the team investigate when naval cryptanalyst Andrew Wells is shot dead in a park outside Baltimore. At the scene, Tony spots a man who was one of his informants from 15 years earlier when he was a police officer, but who went into hiding after a bid to bring down the Russian mobsters he worked for went wrong. Drama, starring Mark Harmon.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs is forced to take a step back from work when his increasingly frail father Jackson begs him to help track down an old friend from the Second World War who does not have long left to live. Meanwhile, the rest of the team investigate the death of a marine sergeant who appears to have been caught in the crossfire during a bungled robbery, but Tony and Tim find evidence the victim may have been targeted. Crime drama, guest starring Ralph Waite (The Waltons), with Mark Harmon.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Petty officer Jody Ray is killed by a hit-and-run driver and evidence points to staff sergeant Justin Dunne, whose guilt seems certain when DiNozzo and McGee find him washing a bloodstained knife when they go to arrest him. However, Dunne tells his lawyer Carrie Clark he was miles away in Falls Church at the time - stabbing bookmaker Donny Sullivan to death. Unable to reveal the truth to the NCIS team, Clark decides to check out the alibi herself. Crime drama, guest starring Salli Richardson-Whitfield (A Town Called Eureka).(n)

programme.title: Ransom
programme.desc: Cynthia and her younger brother are abducted by a criminal , forcing Eric to break all the rules to rescue them.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: The team tries to stop a sex-trafficking ring after a mother goes undercover to find her daughter, who has been taken by the gang, and later Benson uncovers a shocking truth. Hard-hitting and emotional drama, starring Mariska Hargitay.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: The team investigates when the wife of a popular writer is accused of spousal abuse after fighting with her stepdaughters. Hard-hitting drama, starring Mariska Hargitay.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Amaro's behaviour earns him a place back on the squad just in time to investigate Hollywood starlet Tensley Evans' latest offence - the statutory rape of a 15-year-old boy. Benson believes her recent erratic behaviour could have been sparked because she has been a victim of abuse herself.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A disturbed young man with a vendetta against women seeks revenge for being snubbed and bullied in his past, which leaves Rollins in danger as she tries to stop an attack. Elsewhere, Benson tries to find the time to see her son in hospital.(n)

programme.title: Bull
programme.desc: A professional eSports video games player is accused of throwing a championship game, so he enlists Bull to help him sue his former team manager for defamation.(n)

programme.desc: After a woman is electrocuted and killed in her bathtub, Cabot Cove is flooded with pen point letters that turn neighbours against each other. Starring Angela Lansbury, Tom Bosley and John Astin.(n)

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Jessica is called for jury duty in the trial of a murder suspect claiming self-defence, but she realises there is more to the case than meets the eye. Drama, starring Angela Lansbury.(n)

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: When a number of guests become ill following breakfast at Cabot Cove's newest tourist attraction, the popular Joshua Peabody Inn, Jessica suspects foul play. Starring Angela Lansbury and Tom Bosley.(n)

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Jessica is alarmed to learn a friend's son has been arrested for murdering the local bully in a close-knit community, and sets out to clear his name. Drama, starring Angela Lansbury, Craig Stevens and Mariclare Costello.(n)

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Jessica learns her old teacher has been killed shortly after revising her will, putting the new beneficiary - a TV evangelist - under suspicion of committing murder. Starring Angela Lansbury and Richard Herd.(n)

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Jessica investigates a murder connected to the contents of a diary belonging to a movie actress, which was stolen on the night of her death and subsequently put up for auction. Starring Angela Lansbury and Jerry Orbach.(n)

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Jessica attends the funeral of an old friend, whose former lover interrupts the service to insist he did not die of a heart attack, as the medical examiner's report claims, but was actually murdered. Starring Angela Lansbury, Tom Bosley, Carrie Snodgress and Audrey Landers.(n)

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Jessica is upset when she learns that a short story of hers will appear in a magazine that has become known for its racy pictures of young women. programme.title: Merlin

programme.title: The Queen's Palaces
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce looks into the past of Edinburgh's Palace of Holyroodhouse, which is located close to the dormant volcano Arthur's Seat. Events in the royal residence, one of the smallest palaces in Britain, have determined the futures of England, France and Scotland, and it was the last place in which a royal prince challenged the right of an English king to sit on the throne. Fiona also examines objects including the Darnley jewel, which features numerous hidden messages, and the needlework of a doomed queen.(n)

programme.desc: Part one of two. Documentary examining what impact the relationships between cousins Nicholas II of Russia, Kaiser Wilhelm II of Germany and George V of the United Kingdom had on the outbreak of the First World War. programme.title: Strike It Lucky
programme.desc: Michael Barrymore hosts the madcap game show, in which three teams of two prove seek to progress along a board game-style path by answering questions. Each duo are allowed to determine how many spaces they would like to move by answering the corresponding number of questions. Landing on a space reveals it's hidden contents, which could amount to a prize, or a 'hot spot', which causes contestants to forfeit their gathered winnings.(n)
